variants.translation: Hello! All good?

phrase_patterns.translation: I went for a walk on the {levada_name} levada.

variants.text: Gosto imenso de caminhar nas levadas.
variants.translation: I really like walking on the levadas.

phrase_patterns.base: Pois é, a vida aqui é {how_life_is}, não é?
phrase_patterns.translation: That's right, life here is {how_life_is}, isn't it?

dialogues.title: Morning Chat on the Street
dialogues.context: Dona Lúcia and Marco, neighbours, meet on the street near a small grocery store in the morning.

lines.speaker: Dona Lúcia
lines.text: Bom dia, Marco! Tudo bem?
lines.translation: Good morning, Marco! All good?

lines.speaker: Marco
lines.text: Bom dia, Dona Lúcia! Sim, tudo bem, obrigado. E a senhora, como está?
lines.translation: Good morning, Dona Lúcia! Yes, all good, thank you. And you, how are you?

lines.speaker: Dona Lúcia
lines.text: Está-se bem, está-se bem. Este solzinho já ajuda, não é? Ontem esteve um bocado esquisito.
lines.translation: I'm well, I'm well. This little sun already helps, doesn't it? Yesterday it was a bit strange.

lines.speaker: Marco
lines.text: Pois é, o tempo aqui muda num instante. A senhora vai às compras?
lines.translation: That's right, the weather here changes in an instant. Are you going shopping?

lines.speaker: Dona Lúcia
lines.text: Vou sim, preciso de umas semilhas para o jantar. E o Marco, já foi à levada hoje?
lines.translation: Yes, I am, I need some potatoes for dinner. And Marco, have you been to the levada today?

lines.speaker: Marco
lines.text: Ainda não, mas estou a planear ir à tarde. A de São Jorge é sempre bonita nesta altura.
lines.translation: Not yet, but I'm planning to go this afternoon. The São Jorge one is always beautiful at this time of year.

lines.speaker: Dona Lúcia
lines.text: É verdade. Olhe, tenho de ir, que as semilhas não se compram sozinhas! Até logo, Marco!
lines.translation: It's true. Look, I have to go, the potatoes don't buy themselves! See you later, Marco!

lines.speaker: Marco
lines.text: Até logo, Dona Lúcia! Bom dia!
lines.translation: See you later, Dona Lúcia! Good day!

cultural_notes.title: The Importance of Greetings and Small Talk
cultural_notes.body: In Madeiran communities, it's very common and expected to greet neighbours and engage in brief small talk when you meet them on the street or in local shops. This daily interaction is a vital part of social cohesion and belonging. Even a simple 'Bom dia!' or 'Tudo bem?' followed by a quick comment about the weather or daily plans helps maintain community bonds.

cultural_notes.title: Using 'Pois é' for Agreement
cultural_notes.body: 'Pois é' is a very common expression in European Portuguese, especially in informal settings like neighbourhood chats. It's used to express agreement, acknowledgement, or to confirm something that has just been said. It can be translated as 'that's right', 'indeed', or 'it is so'.

roleplay.scenario: You meet your neighbour, Senhor Manuel, near the local minimarket in the morning. He's a friendly older man who enjoys a quick chat.

nodes.npc_text: Bom dia! Como é que está? Está um dia bonito, não está?
nodes.npc_translation: Good morning! How are you? It's a beautiful day, isn't it?

options.text: Bom dia, Senhor Manuel! Estou bem, obrigado. Sim, está um dia ótimo!
options.translation: Good morning, Senhor Manuel! I'm well, thank you. Yes, it's a great day!
options.feedback: Great start! You greeted him back and agreed about the weather politely.

options.text: Olá! Tudo bem. O tempo está bom hoje.
options.translation: Hello! All good. The weather is good today.
options.feedback: Good, a slightly more informal but perfectly acceptable response.

nodes.npc_text: Pois é, um dia bom para ir à levada. Eu já fui, à levada do Rei. E o que vai fazer hoje?
nodes.npc_translation: That's right, a good day to go to the levada. I've already been, to the Levada do Rei. And what are you going to do today?

options.text: Que bom! Eu ainda não fui, mas talvez vá à tarde. Gosto imenso da de São Jorge.
options.translation: How nice! I haven't been yet, but maybe I'll go this afternoon. I really like the São Jorge one.
options.feedback: Excellent, you shared your plans and mentioned a local levada.

options.text: Ah, a levada do Rei é muito bonita! Eu hoje vou só tratar de umas coisas em casa.
options.translation: Ah, the Levada do Rei is very beautiful! Today I'm just going to take care of some things at home.
options.feedback: Good, you acknowledged his activity and shared your own simple plans.

nodes.npc_text: Sim, todas as levadas têm o seu encanto. Olhe, preciso de ir comprar um bolo do caco para o almoço. A gente encontra-se por aí!
nodes.npc_translation: Yes, all the levadas have their charm. Look, I need to go buy a bolo do caco for lunch. We'll see each other around!

options.text: Pois é! Até logo, Senhor Manuel! Bom almoço!
options.translation: That's right! See you later, Senhor Manuel! Have a good lunch!
options.feedback: Perfect! You acknowledged his comment and said a friendly goodbye.

options.text: Sim, concordo. Até logo!
options.translation: Yes, I agree. See you later!
options.feedback: Good, a simple and polite way to end the conversation.

dialogues.title: Ordering at the Arraial
dialogues.context: At a busy food stall during a Madeiran arraial, a local is ordering from a vendor.

lines.speaker: Vendedor
lines.text: Boa noite! O que vai ser?
lines.translation: Good evening! What will it be?

lines.speaker: Local
lines.text: Boa noite! Queria uma espetada e um bolo do caco, por favor.
lines.translation: Good evening! I'd like a beef skewer and a bolo do caco, please.

lines.speaker: Vendedor
lines.text: Com ou sem semilhas?
lines.translation: With or without potatoes?

lines.speaker: Local
lines.text: Ah, sem semilhas, por enquanto. E uma poncha, pode ser?
lines.translation: Ah, without potatoes for now. And a poncha, if possible?

lines.speaker: Vendedor
lines.text: Claro! Fica tudo dezasseis euros. É p'ra levar ou come aqui?
lines.translation: Of course! That's sixteen euros. Is it to go or are you eating here?

lines.speaker: Local
lines.text: Não, é p'ra comer aqui, no meio da confusão!
lines.translation: No, it's to eat here, in the middle of the hustle and bustle!

dialogues.title: Enjoying the Arraial
dialogues.context: Two friends, Ana and Rui, are trying to find a spot to eat their food at a crowded arraial. The general ambience is noisy.

lines.speaker: Ana
lines.text: Ufa, que confusão! Mas a espetada está ótima, não está?
lines.translation: Phew, what a crowd! But the espetada is great, isn't it?

lines.speaker: Rui
lines.text: Está uma delícia! E esta poncha... humm! Precisava mesmo disto.
lines.translation: It's delicious! And this poncha... mmm! I really needed this.

lines.speaker: Ana
lines.text: Pois é. A animação também está boa este ano. Estão a tocar umas músicas fixes.
lines.translation: Indeed. The entertainment is also good this year. They're playing some cool songs.

lines.speaker: Rui
lines.text: Sim, sim! Olha, queres ir ver as barraquinhas de artesanato depois? São por ali.
lines.translation: Yes, yes! Look, do you want to go see the craft stalls later? They're over there.

lines.speaker: Ana
lines.text: Diz? Não ouvi bem! As barraquinhas de quê?
lines.translation: What? I didn't hear well! The stalls of what?

lines.speaker: Rui
lines.text: De artesanato! P'ra ver se há alguma coisa gira.
lines.translation: Crafts! To see if there's anything nice.

lines.speaker: Ana
lines.text: Ah, sim! Boa ideia. Mas primeiro, mais um bocado de espetada!
lines.translation: Ah, yes! Good idea. But first, a bit more espetada!

cultural_notes.title: The Madeiran Arraial
cultural_notes.body: Arraiais are traditional religious festivals in Madeira, often celebrating a patron saint. While they have a strong religious component (masses, processions), they are also major social events. Expect lively music, dancing, and, most importantly, abundant food and drink stalls. They are a fantastic way to experience Madeiran culture and gastronomy. The atmosphere is usually very informal and joyful.

cultural_notes.title: Ordering Food and Drink
cultural_notes.body: At an arraial, food and drink are typically ordered at 'barracas' (stalls). It's common to queue up, place your order, pay, and then find a spot to eat, often standing or at shared tables. Don't be shy to ask 'Com licença' to navigate through crowds. Using 'por favor' and 'obrigado/a' is always polite, even in a bustling environment. Madeirans often use 'semilha' for potato instead of 'batata'.

roleplay.scenario: You're at a busy arraial in Madeira, feeling hungry and thirsty. You decide to approach a food stall to order some typical Madeiran food and a drink.

nodes.npc_text: Boa noite! O que vai ser? Temos espetada, bolo do caco, e semilhas fritas!
nodes.npc_translation: Good evening! What will it be? We have espetada, bolo do caco, and fried potatoes!

options.text: Queria uma espetada, por favor.
options.translation: I'd like an espetada, please.
options.feedback: Great start! You clearly asked for the espetada. Now, what about a drink?

options.text: Olá! Quanto é que custa a espetada?
options.translation: Hello! How much does the espetada cost?
options.feedback: Good, you asked about the price first. Now you can decide to order.

nodes.npc_text: A espetada é dez euros. As semilhas são três. E as bebidas, temos poncha e água.
nodes.npc_translation: The espetada is ten euros. The potatoes are three. And for drinks, we have poncha and water.

options.text: Ok, queria uma espetada e uma poncha, por favor.
options.translation: Okay, I'd like an espetada and a poncha, please.
options.feedback: Excellent! You ordered both food and drink.

options.text: Só a espetada, então. E uma água, se faz favor.
options.translation: Just the espetada, then. And a water, please.
options.feedback: Good choice, you opted for water. Now, time to pay.

nodes.npc_text: Certo. E p'ra beber? Uma poncha, talvez?
nodes.npc_translation: Alright. And to drink? A poncha, perhaps?

options.text: Sim, uma poncha, por favor.
options.translation: Yes, a poncha, please.
options.feedback: Perfeito! You've ordered both your meal and a typical Madeiran drink.

options.text: Não, obrigada. Só a espetada.
options.translation: No, thank you. Just the espetada.
options.feedback: That's fine, you politely declined the drink. Now to the payment.

nodes.npc_text: Fica tudo doze euros e cinquenta. É p'ra levar ou come aqui?
nodes.npc_translation: That's twelve euros fifty. Is it to go or are you eating here?

options.text: É p'ra comer aqui, obrigada.
options.translation: It's to eat here, thank you.
options.feedback: Good, you specified you're eating there. Make sure you pay!

options.text: É p'ra levar, por favor.
options.translation: It's to go, please.
options.feedback: Excellent, you chose to take it away. Now, pay up!

nodes.npc_text: Aqui está! Bom apetite!
nodes.npc_translation: Here you go! Enjoy your meal!

options.text: Obrigada! Está muito bom!
options.translation: Thank you! It's very good!
options.feedback: Great, you expressed your appreciation for the food. Well done!

options.text: Obrigada! Com licença, posso passar?
options.translation: Thank you! Excuse me, may I pass?
options.feedback: Perfect! You thanked the vendor and politely asked to navigate the crowd. Mission accomplished!

dialogues.title: A Morning Chat in the Village
dialogues.context: Maria and João, neighbours in a small Madeiran village, meet by chance near the local café. It's a slightly grey morning.

lines.speaker: Maria
lines.text: Bom dia, João! Que tempo, não é? Parece que está a chover miúdo.
lines.translation: Good morning, João! What weather, isn't it? It looks like it's drizzling lightly.

lines.speaker: João
lines.text: Bom dia, Maria! Pois é, mas é da Madeira. Nunca se sabe! Vais tomar uma bica?
lines.translation: Good morning, Maria! Indeed, but it's from Madeira. You never know! Are you going for a coffee?

lines.speaker: Maria
lines.text: Vou sim. Tenho de ir ao mercado depois, mas com esta chuvinha... que maçada!
lines.translation: Yes, I am. I have to go to the market afterwards, but with this drizzle... what a nuisance!

lines.speaker: João
lines.text: Ah, não te acanhes! É só uma chuvinha. O mercado está coberto. Eu já lá vou também.
lines.translation: Ah, don't be shy! It's just a little rain. The market is covered. I'm going there too.

lines.speaker: Maria
lines.text: Tens razão. Então, até já no mercado!
lines.translation: You're right. So, see you at the market!

cultural_notes.title: Madeiran Weather Talk: 'Chover Miúdo'
cultural_notes.body: 'Está a chover miúdo' is a very common and specific Madeiran expression for light, fine rain or drizzle. While 'chuviscar' is used across Portugal, 'chover miúdo' is a local favourite. It reflects the island's often unpredictable and varied microclimates.

cultural_notes.title: Encouragement and Hospitality: 'Não te acanhes!'
cultural_notes.body: In Portugal, and especially in Madeira, it's common to encourage guests or friends to feel at home and not hold back, particularly when offering food or drink. 'Não te acanhes!' is a warm, informal way to say 'Don't be shy!' or 'Help yourself!', showing genuine hospitality and a desire for you to relax and enjoy.

roleplay.scenario: You're at a small local market stall in Madeira, looking at some fresh fruit. The vendor notices you and offers you a taste of some local produce.

nodes.npc_text: Bom dia! Gostavas de provar esta anona? É da terra, muito fresquinha.
nodes.npc_translation: Good morning! Would you like to try this custard apple? It's local, very fresh.

options.text: Sim, obrigado/a! Parece deliciosa.
options.translation: Yes, thank you! It looks delicious.
options.feedback: Great! You accepted politely and showed enthusiasm. This is a common and welcome response.

options.text: Não, obrigado/a. Só estou a olhar.
options.translation: No, thank you. I'm just looking.
options.feedback: This is polite, but you missed an opportunity to engage with the local culture and hospitality. Vendors often appreciate you trying their produce.

nodes.npc_text: Então, não te acanhes! É mesmo boa. Diz lá o que achas.
nodes.npc_translation: So, don't be shy! It's really good. Tell me what you think.

options.text: Mmm, é fantástica! Muito doce.
options.translation: Mmm, it's fantastic! Very sweet.
options.feedback: Excellent! You tried the fruit and gave positive feedback, engaging naturally with the vendor.

options.text: Obrigado/a pela provinha.
options.translation: Thank you for the taste.
options.feedback: Good, you thanked them. Giving a short opinion ('muito bom', 'delicioso') would be even more engaging.

nodes.npc_text: Pois, como quiseres. Mas se mudares de ideias, é só dizer!
nodes.npc_translation: Well, as you wish. But if you change your mind, just say so!

options.text: Está bem, obrigado/a!
options.translation: Okay, thank you!
options.feedback: You ended the interaction politely. Next time, consider accepting the offer to engage more with local hospitality.

nodes.npc_text: Ainda bem que gostaste! Queres levar uma?
nodes.npc_translation: Glad you liked it! Do you want to take one?

options.text: Sim, por favor! Vou levar duas.
options.translation: Yes, please! I'll take two.
options.feedback: Perfect! You've successfully engaged, appreciated the local produce, and made a purchase.

options.text: Talvez para a próxima. Obrigado/a!
options.translation: Maybe next time. Thank you!
options.feedback: Polite, but a missed opportunity to buy after enjoying the sample. Vendors appreciate it when you buy after tasting.

dialogues.title: Festival Talk
dialogues.context: Two neighbours, Sofia and João, meet by chance at the local market, discussing the upcoming arraial.

lines.speaker: Sofia
lines.text: Bom dia, João! Tudo bem? Estás a ver as semilhas?
lines.translation: Good morning, João! All good? Are you looking at the potatoes?

lines.speaker: João
lines.text: Bom dia, Sofia! Sim, estão com bom aspeto. E tu, já a pensar no arraial do próximo fim de semana?
lines.translation: Good morning, Sofia! Yes, they look good. And you, already thinking about next weekend's festival?

lines.speaker: Sofia
lines.text: Pois é! Estou com imensa vontade de ir. A música ao vivo é sempre boa.
lines.translation: Indeed! I'm really looking forward to going. The live music is always good.

lines.speaker: João
lines.text: É verdade. E a poncha, claro! Que tal irmos juntos, se não tiveres planos?
lines.translation: It's true. And the poncha, of course! How about we go together, if you don't have plans?

lines.speaker: Sofia
lines.text: Era ótimo! Eu acho que o arraial é sempre o melhor para animar. Vamos então?
lines.translation: That would be great! I think the festival is always the best to liven things up. Shall we go then?

lines.speaker: João
lines.text: Combinado. Falamos durante a semana. Até já!
lines.translation: It's a plan. We'll talk during the week. See you!

cultural_notes.title: Arraiais: The Heart of Madeiran Community
cultural_notes.body: Arraiais are traditional local festivals, often linked to religious celebrations, but they are also vibrant social events. They typically feature live music, traditional food (like espetadas and bolo do caco), drinks (especially poncha), and a lively atmosphere. They are a cornerstone of community life in Madeira, bringing neighbours together for fun and celebration. Expressing enthusiasm for an arraial is a common way to connect with locals.

cultural_notes.title: Expressing Opinions and Making Plans Informally
cultural_notes.body: In Madeira, especially with neighbours or friends, conversations are often informal. Using 'tu' is standard. Phrases like 'Eu acho que...' (I think that...) are common for sharing opinions. When suggesting plans, 'Que tal irmos...?' (How about we go...?) is a friendly way to invite someone. Interjections like 'Pois é!' (Indeed!/That's right!) are used to show agreement and keep the conversation flowing naturally.

roleplay.scenario: You're at a local café, enjoying a bica. Your neighbour, Maria, approaches you and starts a conversation about the weekend and a local event.

nodes.npc_text: Olá! Tudo bem? A aproveitar a bica? Já a pensar nos planos para o fim de semana?
nodes.npc_translation: Hello! All good? Enjoying your espresso? Already thinking about plans for the weekend?

options.text: Olá, Maria! Sim, está tudo bem. Ainda não pensei muito, e tu?
options.translation: Hello, Maria! Yes, all good. I haven't thought much yet, and you?
options.feedback: Good start! You greeted her and turned the question back to her.

options.text: Olá! Estou um bocado cansado, mas sim, a bica ajuda. Ainda não sei os planos.
options.translation: Hello! I'm a bit tired, but yes, the espresso helps. I don't know the plans yet.
options.feedback: It's fine to mention being tired. You still answered her question about plans.

nodes.npc_text: Eu também não. Mas ouvi dizer que vai haver um arraial pequeno na freguesia vizinha. Que tal irmos lá no sábado à noite?
nodes.npc_translation: Me neither. But I heard there's going to be a small festival in the next parish. How about we go there on Saturday night?

options.text: Um arraial? Que boa ideia! Eu acho que o arraial é sempre divertido.
options.translation: A festival? What a good idea! I think the festival is always fun.
options.feedback: Great! You showed enthusiasm and used a relevant phrase pattern.

options.text: Não sei. Estou um bocado cansado com a confusão dos arraiais.
options.translation: I don't know. I'm a bit tired with the crowds at festivals.
options.feedback: It's okay to express hesitation. Maria might try to convince you.

nodes.npc_text: Ah, pois é! O trabalho cansa. Mas ouvi dizer que vai haver um arraial pequeno na freguesia vizinha. Que tal irmos lá no sábado à noite? Talvez ajude a descontrair.
nodes.npc_translation: Ah, indeed! Work is tiring. But I heard there's going to be a small festival in the next parish. How about we go there on Saturday night? Maybe it will help you relax.

options.text: Pois, se calhar. Eu acho que a música ao vivo é boa para esquecer o cansaço.
options.translation: Yes, maybe. I think live music is good for forgetting tiredness.
options.feedback: Good, you're considering it and linking it to a positive aspect.

options.text: Não sei, Maria. Estou mesmo sem vontade de ir a sítios com muita gente.
options.translation: I don't know, Maria. I really don't feel like going to places with many people.
options.feedback: You're politely declining. That's a valid option in real life.

nodes.npc_text: Sim, a música e a poncha! É sempre bom. Então, combinado? Encontramo-nos à entrada às nove?
nodes.npc_translation: Yes, the music and the poncha! It's always good. So, it's a plan? Shall we meet at the entrance at nine?

options.text: Sim, combinado! Até sábado então. Obrigada pelo convite!
options.translation: Yes, it's a plan! See you Saturday then. Thanks for the invitation!
options.feedback: Excellent! You confirmed the plan and thanked her politely. Conversation ends well.

nodes.npc_text: Ah, mas este é mais pequeno, não tem tanta gente como os grandes. E a poncha é caseira! Pensa bem.
nodes.npc_translation: Ah, but this one is smaller, it doesn't have as many people as the big ones. And the poncha is homemade! Think about it.

options.text: Hmm, poncha caseira... Isso já me convence mais. Que tal irmos então?
options.translation: Hmm, homemade poncha... That convinces me more. How about we go then?
options.feedback: You're swayed by the details! Good, you're engaging with her arguments.

options.text: Mesmo assim, estou a precisar de um fim de semana mais calmo. Fica para a próxima.
options.translation: Even so, I need a calmer weekend. Maybe next time.
options.feedback: Polite refusal. It's good to know how to decline an invitation.

nodes.npc_text: Percebo perfeitamente. Às vezes precisamos de paz. Então, fica para a próxima. Mas se mudares de ideias, é só dizer!
nodes.npc_translation: I understand perfectly. Sometimes we need peace. So, next time then. But if you change your mind, just say so!

options.text: Obrigada, Maria! Bom arraial para ti. Até breve!
options.translation: Thanks, Maria! Have a good festival. See you soon!
options.feedback: You ended the conversation politely, wishing her well. Well done.

dialogues.title: Restaurant Bill Mistake
dialogues.context: At a restaurant in Funchal, after a meal, the customer notices an extra item on the bill.

lines.speaker: Cliente
lines.text: Desculpe, o senhor podia vir aqui um momento, se faz favor?
lines.translation: Excuse me, could you come here for a moment, please?

lines.speaker: Empregado
lines.text: Pois não, diga. Há algum problema?
lines.translation: Yes, what can I do for you? Is there a problem?

lines.speaker: Cliente
lines.text: Acho que há um engano na fatura. Não me parece que tenhamos pedido esta poncha extra.
lines.translation: I think there's a mistake on the bill. I don't think we ordered this extra poncha.

lines.speaker: Empregado

cultural_notes.title: The Art of Polite Complaint in Portugal
cultural_notes.body: In Portugal, direct confrontation is generally avoided, especially in service interactions. When making a complaint, it's best to start with polite phrases like 'Desculpe' (Excuse me) or 'Com licença' (With your permission), followed by an indirect phrasing like 'Acho que há um engano...' (I think there's a mistake...) or 'Não estou a perceber...' (I'm not understanding...). Frame it as a question or an observation, rather than an accusation. Using the conditional ('poderia', 'gostaria') or imperfect ('queria') tenses makes requests softer and more polite. Maintaining a calm and respectful tone is key to achieving a positive outcome.

cultural_notes.title: Addressing Service Staff
cultural_notes.body: When addressing service staff, it's common to use 'o senhor' / 'a senhora' (the sir / the madam) as a polite form of address, especially if you don't know the person. This is more common and less distancing than 'você'. For example, 'O senhor podia ajudar-me?' (Could you help me, sir?) or 'A senhora sabe dizer-me...?' (Do you know how to tell me..., madam?). This shows respect and facilitates a good interaction, even when raising an issue.

roleplay.scenario: You've just received a delivery from a local grocery store in Madeira, but one of the items you ordered, a specific type of Madeiran banana, is missing from the bag, although it's on the receipt. You need to call the store to sort it out.

nodes.npc_text: Bom dia, Loja da Esquina, como posso ajudar?
nodes.npc_translation: Good morning, Corner Shop, how can I help?

options.text: Bom dia. Desculpe, mas acho que há um engano com a minha entrega.
options.translation: Good morning. Excuse me, but I think there's a mistake with my delivery.
options.feedback: Excellent start! You were polite and clear about the issue.

options.text: Olá. Recebi a minha encomenda e falta uma coisa.
options.translation: Hello. I received my order and something is missing.
options.feedback: A bit direct, but still understandable. Try to be a little more indirect next time.

nodes.npc_text: Pois não, diga. Qual é o problema com a entrega? Tem o número do pedido?
nodes.npc_translation: Yes, what can I do for you? What's the problem with the delivery? Do you have the order number?

options.text: Sim, o número é 12345. Pelo que vejo na fatura, paguei pelas bananas da Madeira, mas elas não vieram.
options.translation: Yes, the number is 12345. From what I see on the bill, I paid for the Madeiran bananas, but they didn't come.
options.feedback: Very good! You provided the necessary information and stated the problem clearly and politely.

options.text: Falta-me um cacho de bananas da Madeira. O meu número é 12345.
options.translation: I'm missing a bunch of Madeiran bananas. My number is 12345.
options.feedback: Clear and concise. Adding 'por favor' or a softer opening could make it even better.

nodes.npc_text: Certo. E o que é que falta, exatamente? Tem o número da sua encomenda?
nodes.npc_translation: Okay. And what exactly is missing? Do you have your order number?

options.text: Sim, o número é 12345. Não recebi as bananas da Madeira, mas estão na fatura.
options.translation: Yes, the number is 12345. I didn't receive the Madeiran bananas, but they are on the bill.
options.feedback: You clearly stated the problem and provided the order number. Good job.

nodes.npc_text: Ah, peço imensa desculpa por isso! Vou verificar aqui. Pois é, parece que houve um lapso. Quer que enviemos as bananas ou prefere que descontemos o valor na próxima compra?
nodes.npc_translation: Ah, I am so sorry about that! I'll check here. Yes, it seems there was an oversight. Would you like us to send the bananas or would you prefer us to discount the amount on your next purchase?

options.text: Se não for muito incómodo, preferia que enviassem as bananas, se faz favor.
options.translation: If it's not too much trouble, I'd prefer if you sent the bananas, please.
options.feedback: Polite and clear preference. Using 'se não for muito incómodo' is very good.

options.text: Acho que prefiro que enviem as bananas, sim.
options.translation: I think I prefer that you send the bananas, yes.
options.feedback: Clear choice. A little more politeness could be added, but still acceptable.

nodes.npc_text: Certo, vamos enviar as bananas o mais rapidamente possível. Devem chegar dentro de uma hora. Mais uma vez, pedimos desculpa pelo sucedido.
nodes.npc_translation: Okay, we'll send the bananas as quickly as possible. They should arrive within an hour. Once again, we apologize for what happened.

options.text: Muito obrigado pela sua ajuda. Tenha um bom dia.
options.translation: Thank you very much for your help. Have a good day.
options.feedback: Perfect ending! You thanked them and ended the call politely.

sit-m5w2-negotiating-arranging.title: Negotiating & Arranging

dialogues.title: Plumber Quote
dialogues.context: A client is calling a plumber to fix a leaky tap and discuss the cost and timing for the repair.

lines.speaker: Cliente
lines.text: Bom dia. É da canalização, certo? Tenho uma torneira a pingar na cozinha.
lines.translation: Good morning. Is this the plumbing service, right? I have a tap dripping in the kitchen.

lines.speaker: Canalizador
lines.text: Bom dia! Sim, sou eu. Diga. Onde é que é o problema?
lines.translation: Good morning! Yes, it's me. Tell me. Where's the problem?

lines.speaker: Cliente
lines.text: É na cozinha. Quanto é que fica o arranjo? E quando é que consegue vir?
lines.translation: It's in the kitchen. How much will the repair be? And when can you come?

lines.speaker: Canalizador
lines.text: Olhe, só vendo no local. Mas p'ra já, posso ir aí amanhã de manhã, se for bem.
lines.translation: Look, I can only tell by seeing it on site. But for now, I can go there tomorrow morning, if that's fine.

lines.speaker: Cliente
lines.text: Amanhã de manhã, sim, pode ser. Mas preciso de ter uma ideia do preço, pelo menos aproximado.
lines.translation: Tomorrow morning, yes, that can work. But I need to have an idea of the price, at least approximate.

lines.speaker: Canalizador
lines.text: Pois, um arranjo desses, com material e tudo, deve ficar entre os 60 e os 80 euros. Mas só confirmo lá.
lines.translation: Well, a repair like that, with material and everything, should be between 60 and 80 euros. But I'll only confirm there.

lines.speaker: Cliente
lines.text: Certo. E se ficar um bocado mais caro, podemos negociar?
lines.translation: Okay. And if it's a bit more expensive, can we negotiate?

lines.speaker: Canalizador
lines.text: Pois, sempre se pode falar. Amanhã de manhã, então. Ficamos combinados.
lines.translation: Well, we can always talk. Tomorrow morning, then. It's settled.

cultural_notes.title: Negotiating Service Prices
cultural_notes.body: In Portugal, especially with independent tradespeople (like plumbers, electricians, handymen), it's often acceptable to politely try and negotiate the price for a service. It's not considered rude to ask for a 'melhor preço' (better price) or to discuss the quote. This is less common in larger companies or fixed-price retail settings.

cultural_notes.title: Time and Deadlines
cultural_notes.body: When arranging times with tradespeople, 'amanhã de manhã' (tomorrow morning) can sometimes mean 'sometime in the morning' rather than a precise hour. It's common to confirm the day and then re-confirm closer to the time, or to ask for a narrower time window if precision is important to you. Deadlines (`prazos`) can also be a bit flexible, so clear communication is key.

roleplay.scenario: You've asked a local gardener to give you a quote for trimming your hedge and cleaning your garden. The price is a bit higher than you expected, and you also need the work done by a specific day.

nodes.npc_text: Então, para podar as sebes todas e limpar o jardim, o orçamento fica em 180 euros. É um trabalho que leva um dia inteiro.
nodes.npc_translation: So, to trim all the hedges and clean the garden, the quote is 180 euros. It's a full day's work.

options.text: É um bocado caro. Não consegue fazer um preço melhor?
options.translation: It's a bit expensive. Can't you make a better price?
options.feedback: You've politely expressed concern about the price and asked for a discount. Good start!

options.text: O preço é aceitável, mas preciso que esteja feito até sexta-feira.
options.translation: The price is acceptable, but I need it to be done by Friday.
options.feedback: You've accepted the price but immediately set a condition for the deadline. Clear communication!

options.text: E se fizermos só a parte da frente? Quanto é que fica?
options.translation: What if we only do the front part? How much would that be?
options.feedback: You're proposing a compromise by reducing the scope of work to lower the cost. Good strategy!

nodes.npc_text: Olhe, o material e o transporte levam o seu custo. Posso fazer por 170 euros, mas menos que isso, não consigo. O trabalho é muito.
nodes.npc_translation: Look, the material and transport have their cost. I can do it for 170 euros, but less than that, I can't. It's a lot of work.

options.text: Certo, entendo. E se fizermos só a parte da frente? Quanto é que fica?
options.translation: Okay, I understand. What if we only do the front part? How much would that be?
options.feedback: You're pivoting to a different negotiation strategy by reducing the work scope.

options.text: OK, aceito os 170 euros, mas preciso de ter a certeza que fica pronto até sexta.
options.translation: Okay, I accept the 170 euros, but I need to be sure it's ready by Friday.
options.feedback: You've accepted the slightly reduced price and are now focusing on the deadline.

options.text: Hmm, mesmo assim, é muito. Agradeço, mas vou ter de procurar outra pessoa.
options.translation: Hmm, even so, it's too much. Thank you, but I'll have to look for someone else.
options.feedback: You've politely declined the offer. Sometimes, walking away is part of negotiation.

nodes.npc_text: Até sexta? Sim, consigo. Posso ir lá na quinta-feira, assim tenho tempo de acabar tudo com calma.
nodes.npc_translation: By Friday? Yes, I can. I can go there on Thursday, so I have time to finish everything calmly.

options.text: Perfeito! Então ficamos combinados para quinta-feira.
options.translation: Perfect! So it's settled for Thursday.
options.feedback: You've successfully agreed on a deadline. Well done!

options.text: E se não conseguir acabar até lá? O que acontece?
options.translation: And if you can't finish by then? What happens?
options.feedback: You're asking for clarification on what happens if the deadline is missed. Important for managing expectations.

nodes.npc_text: Só a parte da frente? Para podar e limpar essa zona, consigo fazer por 90 euros. Demora umas 4 horas.
nodes.npc_translation: Just the front part? To trim and clean that area, I can do it for 90 euros. It takes about 4 hours.

options.text: Esse preço parece-me bem para essa parte. Quando é que pode começar?
options.translation: That price seems good for that part. When can you start?
options.feedback: You've agreed to the reduced scope and price, now asking for a start date.

options.text: Mas se fizer tudo, consegue fazer um desconto maior no total?
options.translation: But if you do everything, can you give a bigger discount on the total?
options.feedback: You're trying to leverage the full scope for a better overall price. Good move!

nodes.npc_text: Pois, se por algum motivo não der, ligo-lhe logo. Mas sou de palavra, não se preocupe. Faço sempre os meus prazos.
nodes.npc_translation: Well, if for some reason it doesn't work out, I'll call you right away. But I keep my word, don't worry. I always meet my deadlines.

options.text: Pois, não queria que isso acontecesse. É importante que esteja pronto.
options.translation: Well, I wouldn't want that to happen. It's important that it's ready.
options.feedback: You've reinforced the importance of the deadline and accepted the verbal assurance.

options.text: Acho que não vamos chegar a um acordo. Obrigado na mesma.
options.translation: I don't think we'll reach an agreement. Thanks anyway.
options.feedback: You've decided not to proceed due to uncertainty about the deadline. This is a valid choice.

nodes.npc_text: Posso ir já amanhã de manhã, se quiser. Ou na quarta-feira.
nodes.npc_translation: I can go tomorrow morning, if you want. Or on Wednesday.

options.text: Ótimo! Amanhã de manhã, então. Ficamos combinados.
options.translation: Great! Tomorrow morning, then. It's settled.
options.feedback: You've successfully set a start date and confirmed the agreement. Well done!

nodes.npc_text: Para o trabalho todo, o melhor que consigo é mesmo os 170 euros, como lhe disse. É o meu preço justo.
nodes.npc_translation: For the whole job, the best I can do is really 170 euros, as I told you. It's my fair price.

options.text: OK, aceito os 170 euros para o trabalho completo. Mas preciso da sua garantia para o prazo até sexta.
options.translation: Okay, I accept the 170 euros for the complete job. But I need your guarantee for the deadline by Friday.
options.feedback: You've accepted the full scope price, but are now pushing for a firm deadline.

options.text: Não, não consigo. Vou ter de ficar só com a parte da frente pelo preço de 90 euros.
options.translation: No, I can't. I'll have to stick with just the front part for the 90 euro price.
options.feedback: You've decided to stick to the smaller scope of work. You know your budget!

nodes.npc_text: Muito bem, então! Até lá. Obrigado.
nodes.npc_translation: Very good, then! See you. Thank you.

nodes.npc_text: Certo, entendo. Se mudar de ideias, é só ligar. Bom dia.
nodes.npc_translation: Okay, I understand. If you change your mind, just call. Good day.

dialogues.title: At the Market Stall
dialogues.context: A tourist is buying vegetables at a market stall in Funchal. There's a slight misunderstanding about the quantity.

lines.speaker: Vendedor
lines.text: Diga! Precisa de ajuda?
lines.translation: Yes! Do you need help?

lines.speaker: Turista
lines.text: Sim, por favor. Queria dois quilos de semilhas.
lines.translation: Yes, please. I'd like two kilos of potatoes.

lines.speaker: Vendedor
lines.text: Dois quilos de cenouras, certo?
lines.translation: Two kilos of carrots, right?

lines.speaker: Turista
lines.text: Desculpe, não percebi. Quis dizer semilhas, não cenouras.
lines.translation: Sorry, I didn't understand. I meant potatoes, not carrots.

lines.speaker: Vendedor

lines.speaker: Turista
lines.text: Obrigado.
lines.translation: Thank you.

cultural_notes.title: Politeness and Clarification
cultural_notes.body: When clarifying a misunderstanding in Portuguese, it's always polite to start with 'Desculpe' (Sorry/Excuse me) or 'Peço desculpa' (I apologize). Phrases like 'Não percebi' (I didn't understand) or 'Pode repetir?' (Can you repeat?) are direct but softened by 'por favor' (please). It's common to use 'Pois' or 'Ah, pois' to acknowledge understanding after a clarification, indicating agreement or realization.

cultural_notes.title: Madeiran Localisms
cultural_notes.body: In Madeira, 'semilhas' is the common word for potatoes, whereas in mainland Portugal, 'batatas' is more prevalent. Using local vocabulary like this shows respect for the local culture and will be appreciated. Also, 'P'ra já!' (short for 'Para já!') is a common, quick way for service staff to say 'Coming right up!' or 'Immediately!'.

phrase_patterns.base: Não estou a perceber o que se {passa}.

variants.translation: I'm not understanding what's happening with this situation.

variants.translation: I'd like to resolve this situation as quickly as possible.

dialogues.title: High Water Bill
dialogues.context: At the Águas da Madeira (Water Company) office, a customer is trying to understand an unusually high water bill.

lines.speaker: Atendente
lines.text: Bom dia. Diga, em que posso ajudar?
lines.translation: Good morning. Tell me, how can I help?

lines.speaker: Cliente
lines.text: Bom dia. É sobre a minha fatura da água. Recebi esta aqui, e o valor está muito mais alto do que o normal. Não estou a perceber o que se passa.
lines.translation: Good morning. It's about my water bill. I received this one here, and the amount is much higher than usual. I'm not understanding what's happening.

lines.speaker: Atendente
lines.text: Pois, entendo. Pode dar-me o seu número de cliente, por favor?
lines.translation: Right, I understand. Can you give me your client number, please?

lines.speaker: Cliente
lines.text: Sim, claro. É o 123456789. Eu costumo pagar tipo 20 euros, e esta veio a 80! Houve algum engano?
lines.translation: Yes, of course. It's 123456789. I usually pay around 20 euros, and this one came to 80! Was there some mistake?

lines.speaker: Atendente
lines.text: Deixe-me verificar... Ah, sim. Pelos registos, parece que houve um consumo bastante elevado neste mês. O contador foi lido no dia 15.
lines.translation: Let me check... Ah, yes. According to the records, it seems there was a very high consumption this month. The meter was read on the 15th.

lines.speaker: Cliente
lines.text: Consumo elevado? Mas não estivemos a usar mais água do que o normal. Será que a leitura está correta? Gostaria de resolver esta situação.
lines.translation: High consumption? But we weren't using more water than usual. Is the reading correct? I'd like to resolve this situation.

lines.speaker: Atendente
lines.text: Posso abrir um processo de verificação da leitura ou podemos agendar a visita de um técnico para inspecionar o contador. Qual prefere?
lines.translation: I can open a process to verify the reading, or we can schedule a technician's visit to inspect the meter. Which do you prefer?

cultural_notes.title: Politeness in Complaints
cultural_notes.body: When making a complaint or raising an issue in Portugal, especially in official settings, it's crucial to maintain a polite and respectful tone. Using phrases like 'Gostaria de...' (I would like to...), 'Será que podemos...' (Can we...), and 'Por favor' (Please) softens your request and is more likely to yield a positive response than an aggressive or demanding approach. Even if you're frustrated, a calm demeanor is always preferred.

cultural_notes.title: Patience with Bureaucracy
cultural_notes.body: Dealing with bureaucracy in Portugal can sometimes be a slow process. It's common for things to take longer than expected, and you might need to visit an office multiple times or wait for documents. Patience and persistence, combined with politeness, are key. Avoid expecting immediate resolutions, and be prepared to follow up if necessary.

roleplay.scenario: You've received an electricity bill from EEM (Empresa de Eletricidade da Madeira) that seems unusually high, even though your consumption habits haven't changed. You go to their customer service office to clarify and hopefully resolve the issue.

nodes.npc_text: Bom dia. Diga, em que posso ajudar hoje?
nodes.npc_translation: Good morning. Tell me, how can I help today?

options.text: Bom dia. É sobre a minha fatura da eletricidade. Parece que houve um engano com o valor.
options.translation: Good morning. It's about my electricity bill. It seems there was a mistake with the amount.
options.feedback: Excellent start! You clearly state the purpose of your visit while remaining polite.

options.text: A minha conta da luz está errada. O que se passa?
options.translation: My electricity bill is wrong. What's happening?
options.feedback: This is direct, but a bit abrupt. Remember to use more polite phrasing for official complaints.

nodes.npc_text: Pois, entendo. Pode dar-me o seu número de cliente ou o número da fatura, por favor?
nodes.npc_translation: Right, I understand. Can you give me your client number or the invoice number, please?

options.text: Sim, claro. O número de cliente é o 987654321 e o da fatura é este.
options.translation: Yes, of course. The client number is 987654321 and the invoice number is this one.
options.feedback: Good, providing the requested information promptly helps move the process along.

nodes.npc_text: Obrigado. Deixe-me verificar... Ah, sim. Vejo que a fatura de este mês é de 120 euros. Pelos nossos registos, o consumo parece estar correto, com base na leitura do contador.
nodes.npc_translation: Thank you. Let me check... Ah, yes. I see this month's bill is 120 euros. According to our records, the consumption seems correct, based on the meter reading.

options.text: Mas isso não é possível! O meu consumo nunca foi tão alto. Não estou a perceber o que se passa.
options.translation: But that's not possible! My consumption has never been so high. I'm not understanding what's happening.
options.feedback: Good, you're expressing doubt and asking for clarification, which is key in a misunderstanding.

options.text: Será que a leitura do contador pode estar errada? Gostaria de resolver esta situação.
options.translation: Could the meter reading be wrong? I'd like to resolve this situation.
options.feedback: Excellent, you're politely questioning the data and stating your intention to resolve it.

nodes.npc_text: Pois, os nossos técnicos fizeram a leitura no dia 20. Por vezes, um eletrodoméstico avariado ou uma fuga podem causar um aumento. Será que podemos agendar uma visita técnica para verificar o contador e a instalação?
nodes.npc_translation: Well, our technicians took the reading on the 20th. Sometimes, a faulty appliance or a leak can cause an increase. Can we schedule a technician's visit to check the meter and the installation?

options.text: Sim, por favor. Isso seria ótimo. E se o problema não for meu, o que acontece?
options.translation: Yes, please. That would be great. And if the problem isn't mine, what happens?
options.feedback: You're accepting the proposed solution and also thinking ahead about potential outcomes.

options.text: Não tenho a certeza. Será que podemos encontrar uma solução sem ter de agendar uma visita?
options.translation: I'm not sure. Can we find a solution without having to schedule a visit?
options.feedback: You're trying to negotiate, which is good for L4. The agent might insist on the visit for verification.

nodes.npc_text: Se o técnico verificar que o contador está avariado ou a leitura foi incorreta, faremos o acerto na sua próxima fatura. Se for um problema na sua instalação, ele poderá aconselhá-lo sobre o que fazer. Qual é a sua disponibilidade para a visita?
nodes.npc_translation: If the technician verifies that the meter is faulty or the reading was incorrect, we will adjust your next bill. If it's a problem with your installation, he can advise you on what to do. What is your availability for the visit?

options.text: P'ra já, preciso de ver a minha agenda. Posso ligar mais tarde para agendar?
options.translation: For now, I need to check my schedule. Can I call back later to schedule?
options.feedback: A practical response. You're taking control of the next step.

options.text: Entendido. Muito obrigado pela sua ajuda. Vou aguardar a visita.
options.translation: Understood. Thank you very much for your help. I will await the visit.
options.feedback: A polite and conclusive response, showing you've understood the process.

dialogues.title: A Story from the Market
dialogues.context: Friends catching up at a café in Funchal, one recounts an unusual experience at the market.

lines.speaker: Sofia
lines.text: Olá, Joana! Como é que é? Não vais acreditar no que me aconteceu no Mercado dos Lavradores no outro dia!
lines.translation: Hi, Joana! How are things? You won't believe what happened to me at the Farmers' Market the other day!

lines.speaker: Joana
lines.text: Diz lá! Estou curiosa. Alguma coisa daquelas que só acontecem contigo?
lines.translation: Tell me! I'm curious. Something like those things that only happen to you?

lines.speaker: Sofia
lines.text: Pois é! Eu estava a escolher umas flores, distraída, quando de repente sinto um puxão na mala. Virei-me e era um macaquinho que tinha fugido de um senhor que vendia fruta lá perto!
lines.translation: Exactly! I was choosing some flowers, distracted, when suddenly I felt a tug on my bag. I turned around and it was a little monkey that had escaped from a man selling fruit nearby!

lines.speaker: Joana
lines.text: Um macaquinho? Estás a brincar! E o que é que ele queria?
lines.translation: A little monkey? You're kidding! And what did he want?

lines.speaker: Sofia
lines.text: Ele agarrou numa banana que eu tinha acabado de comprar! O senhor veio a correr, envergonhado, a pedir desculpa. Foi uma situação um bocado hilariante, mas no fim tudo se resolveu. O macaquinho devolveu a banana depois de uma dentada!
lines.translation: He grabbed a banana I had just bought! The man came running, embarrassed, apologizing. It was a bit of a hilarious situation, but in the end everything was resolved. The little monkey returned the banana after a bite!

lines.speaker: Joana
lines.text: Ahaha, imagino a cena! Fico sem palavras! Só mesmo contigo! E o senhor, ficou muito chateado?
lines.translation: Haha, I can imagine the scene! I'm speechless! Only with you! And the man, was he very upset?

lines.speaker: Sofia
lines.text: Não, ele acabou por rir também. Disse que o macaquinho era um artista e que já não era a primeira vez. Ofereceu-me umas semilhas para compensar. Pois, foi um dia diferente!
lines.translation: No, he ended up laughing too. He said the little monkey was an artist and it wasn't the first time. He offered me some potatoes to compensate. Well, it was a different day!

cultural_notes.title: The Art of Storytelling in Portugal
cultural_notes.body: Portuguese conversations often involve recounting personal anecdotes. When telling a story, it's common to use vivid descriptions, express emotions, and build anticipation. Listeners are expected to interject with expressions of surprise, agreement, or curiosity ('Pois!', 'Não me digas!', 'A sério?'). This interaction is a key part of showing engagement and making the story more dynamic.

cultural_notes.title: Using Past Tenses for Narrative
cultural_notes.body: To tell a story effectively in Portuguese, you'll primarily use the 'pretérito imperfeito' (imperfect) for background, ongoing actions, and descriptions in the past ('Eu estava a fazer...', 'Era um dia bonito...'), and the 'pretérito perfeito simples' (simple past) for specific, completed actions that drive the narrative forward ('Senti um puxão', 'Virei-me', 'Ele agarrou'). Mastering the distinction between these two is crucial for recounting events clearly.

roleplay.scenario: You're at a casual get-together with friends in Funchal. Your friend, Miguel, asks if anything interesting has happened to you recently. You need to tell him a story about an unexpected encounter.

nodes.npc_text: Então, como é que é, [Your Name]? Tens novidades? Alguma coisa engraçada ou inesperada que te tenha acontecido ultimamente?
nodes.npc_translation: So, how are things, [Your Name]? Any news? Anything funny or unexpected that's happened to you lately?

options.text: Pois, Miguel, não vais acreditar no que me aconteceu na levada no fim de semana!
options.translation: Well, Miguel, you won't believe what happened to me on the levada this weekend!
options.feedback: Great start! You've grabbed his attention and set the scene for an unexpected story.

options.text: Hmm, deixa-me pensar... Ah, sim, tenho uma história curiosa de quando fui ao Funchal.
options.translation: Hmm, let me think... Oh, yes, I have a curious story from when I went to Funchal.
options.feedback: Good, you've acknowledged the question and are preparing to tell a story.

nodes.npc_text: Conta lá! Estou a ficar curioso. O que é que se passou?
nodes.npc_translation: Tell me! I'm getting curious. What happened?

options.text: Eu estava a caminhar tranquilamente pela levada, a apreciar a vista, quando de repente ouvi um barulho estranho atrás de mim.
options.translation: I was walking peacefully along the levada, enjoying the view, when suddenly I heard a strange noise behind me.
options.feedback: Excellent use of the imperfect for background and simple past for the sudden event. Very descriptive!

options.text: Estava na cidade, a comprar um bolo do caco, e de repente uma senhora começou a falar comigo como se me conhecesse.
options.translation: I was in the city, buying a bolo do caco, and suddenly a lady started talking to me as if she knew me.
options.feedback: Good, you've set up an intriguing scenario. Keep it going!

nodes.npc_text: Um barulho estranho? Ou uma senhora? Não me digas! E então?
nodes.npc_translation: A strange noise? Or a lady? Don't tell me! And then?

options.text: Virei-me e era um cão enorme, mas muito simpático, que me tinha seguido. O dono dele apareceu depois, a rir, a dizer que ele era um 'explorador'.
options.translation: I turned around and it was a huge, but very friendly, dog that had followed me. His owner appeared afterwards, laughing, saying he was an 'explorer'.
options.feedback: You've resolved the mystery and added a touch of humor. Good detail with the owner's comment.

options.text: A senhora insistia que éramos colegas de escola, mas eu nunca a tinha visto na vida! Foi um bocado embaraçoso, mas ela era tão simpática que acabei por rir.
options.translation: The lady insisted we were schoolmates, but I had never seen her in my life! It was a bit embarrassing, but she was so nice that I ended up laughing.
options.feedback: Excellent description of an awkward but ultimately pleasant situation. Good use of 'um bocado'.

nodes.npc_text: Que história! Imagino a tua cara! Foi uma situação um bocado inesperada, não foi?
nodes.npc_translation: What a story! I can imagine your face! It was a bit of an unexpected situation, wasn't it?

options.text: Sim, foi mesmo! Mas foi engraçado. E tu, Miguel, tens alguma história curiosa para contar dos teus dias?
options.translation: Yes, it really was! But it was funny. And you, Miguel, do you have any curious story to tell from your days?
options.feedback: Perfect! You've concluded your story and skillfully invited him to share his own, showing good conversational flow.

options.text: Pois é! Fiquei sem palavras por um momento. Mas agora é uma boa história para contar, não achas?
options.translation: Exactly! I was speechless for a moment. But now it's a good story to tell, don't you think?
options.feedback: You've summarized your feelings and invited his agreement, which is a good way to wrap up. Now, let him talk!

nodes.npc_text: Pois é, é uma ótima história! Por acaso, agora que falas nisso, lembrei-me de uma coisa que me aconteceu na semana passada...
nodes.npc_translation: Indeed, it's a great story! By the way, now that you mention it, I remembered something that happened to me last week...

dialogues.title: Scheduling a Meeting by Phone
dialogues.context: A new resident calls an administrative office in Funchal to schedule a follow-up meeting about a document.

lines.speaker: Rececionista
lines.text: Serviço de Atendimento da Câmara Municipal do Funchal. Diga!
lines.translation: Funchal City Council Customer Service. Yes, tell me!

lines.speaker: Utente
lines.text: Bom dia. O meu nome é [Nome do Utente] e gostaria de agendar uma reunião com o Dr. Silva, por favor.
lines.translation: Good morning. My name is [User Name] and I would like to schedule a meeting with Dr. Silva, please.

lines.speaker: Rececionista
lines.text: Dr. Silva... Um momento, por favor. Qual é o assunto?
lines.translation: Dr. Silva... One moment, please. What is the subject?

lines.speaker: Utente
lines.text: É sobre o processo do meu registo de residência, que ficou pendente.
lines.translation: It's about my residence registration process, which was pending.

lines.speaker: Rececionista

lines.translation: Ah, yes. Dr. Silva has availability next Tuesday, at ten in the morning, or Thursday, at three in the afternoon. Which do you prefer?

lines.speaker: Utente
lines.text: Terça-feira, às dez, seria perfeito. Muito obrigado!
lines.translation: Tuesday, at ten, would be perfect. Thank you very much!

lines.speaker: Rececionista
lines.text: P'ra já! Fica agendado. Tenha um bom dia.
lines.translation: You're welcome! It's scheduled. Have a good day.

lines.speaker: Utente
lines.text: Igualmente.
lines.translation: You too.

cultural_notes.title: Addressing Officials and Formalities
cultural_notes.body: In Portuguese formal settings, especially with public services or higher-ranking professionals, it's common to use 'o Dr.' or 'a Dra.' (Doctor) even if the person is not a medical doctor, but holds a university degree (e.g., lawyer, engineer, manager). Always use 'o senhor' or 'a senhora' when addressing someone formally, and 'por favor' and 'muito obrigado/a' frequently. Email closings like 'Com os melhores cumprimentos' or 'Atenciosamente' are standard.

cultural_notes.title: Phone Etiquette in Offices
cultural_notes.body: When calling an office in Portugal, expect a direct 'Diga!' (Tell me!) or 'Sim?' (Yes?) from the person answering, which is not rude but a standard way to invite you to speak. Be ready to state your name and purpose clearly. Don't be surprised by quick speech; it's common, but they will usually slow down if you ask. Always end with a polite closing like 'Obrigado/a' and 'Tenha um bom dia'.

roleplay.scenario: You need to send a formal email to your building administrator (o administrador do condomínio) about a recurring issue with the building's main entrance door not closing properly. You also want to suggest a time for a meeting.

nodes.npc_text: Olá! Já tens o rascunho do email para o administrador? Deixa-me ver se está tudo bem formal e claro.
nodes.npc_translation: Hi! Do you have the draft email for the administrator? Let me see if it's all formal and clear.

options.text: Sim, aqui está: 'Caro Sr. Administrador, a porta está avariada. Precisa de reparação. Obrigado.'
options.translation: Yes, here it is: 'Dear Mr. Administrator, the door is broken. It needs repair. Thanks.'
options.feedback: Hum... é um bocado direto. Talvez pudesses ser mais formal e explicar melhor a situação. E o tratamento? Ele é 'Dr.' ou 'Exmo. Senhor'?

options.text: Sim, tenho aqui. Pensei em começar com: 'Exmo. Senhor Administrador, venho por este meio comunicar uma anomalia na porta de entrada do edifício.'
options.translation: Yes, I have it here. I thought of starting with: 'Dear Sir Administrator, I hereby wish to communicate an anomaly with the building's entrance door.'
options.feedback: Muito bem! Excelente início. Agora, como é que vais descrever o problema e sugerir uma solução?

nodes.npc_text: Ok, vamos tentar de novo. Como é que farias o pedido de forma mais formal e completa, sem ser demasiado abrupto?
nodes.npc_translation: Okay, let's try again. How would you make the request more formal and complete, without being too abrupt?

options.text: Exmo. Senhor Administrador, venho por este meio comunicar uma anomalia na porta de entrada do edifício, que não está a fechar devidamente.
options.translation: Dear Sir Administrator, I hereby wish to communicate an anomaly with the building's entrance door, which is not closing properly.
options.feedback: Perfeito! Muito mais adequado. Agora, o que sugeres para resolver e como propões uma reunião?

options.text: Olá, a porta continua estragada. Quando é que a vão arranjar?
options.translation: Hi, the door is still broken. When are they going to fix it?
options.feedback: Ainda não está lá. Lembra-te que é uma comunicação formal, para o administrador do condomínio. Precisas de usar um registo mais elevado.

nodes.npc_text: Excelente. Agora, como é que descreves o problema de forma clara e sugere uma possível reunião para discutir a reparação, sem impor a tua agenda?
nodes.npc_translation: Excellent. Now, how do you describe the problem clearly and suggest a possible meeting to discuss the repair, without imposing your agenda?

options.text: Esta situação está a causar transtornos e gostaríamos de agendar uma reunião consigo para debater a solução o mais rapidamente possível.
options.translation: This situation is causing inconvenience and we would like to schedule a meeting with you to discuss the solution as soon as possible.
options.feedback: Quase lá! 'Gostaríamos' é bom, mas talvez pudesses ser um pouco mais flexível com o agendamento. E a despedida?

options.text: A porta não fecha e é preciso arranjar. Podemos falar na terça-feira?
options.translation: The door doesn't close and needs fixing. Can we talk on Tuesday?
options.feedback: Ainda não. Lembra-te da formalidade. Tenta usar 'gostaria de' ou 'seria possível' e uma despedida formal.

nodes.npc_text: Muito bem. Agora, como podes sugerir a reunião de forma mais diplomática e fechar o email formalmente?
nodes.npc_translation: Very good. Now, how can you suggest the meeting more diplomatically and close the email formally?

options.text: Agradeço a sua atenção para este assunto e gostaria de saber da sua disponibilidade para uma breve reunião, a fim de discutir as opções de reparação. Com os melhores cumprimentos,
options.translation: I thank you for your attention to this matter and would like to know your availability for a brief meeting, in order to discuss repair options. With best regards,
options.feedback: Perfeito! É um email muito profissional e cortês. Ele vai apreciar a clareza e o tom. Podes enviar!

options.text: Espero que a porta seja arranjada. Fico à espera. Abraço,
options.translation: I hope the door gets fixed. I'll wait. Hug,
options.feedback: Não, não, 'abraço' é demasiado informal para um administrador! E o tom ainda é um pouco exigente. Tenta ser mais colaborativo e usa uma despedida formal.

nodes.npc_text: Excelente! Este email está impecável. Tenho a certeza que o administrador vai dar a devida atenção. Bom trabalho!
nodes.npc_translation: Excellent! This email is impeccable. I'm sure the administrator will give it due attention. Good job!

sit-m6w3-debating-opinions.summary: Discussing a real topic with a Madeiran: agreeing, disagreeing, conceding and holding your ground with nuance.

variants.translation: I think {topic} is {adjective}.
variants.note: A softer way to express an opinion.

dialogues.title: Cruise Terminal Expansion
dialogues.context: Ana and Carlos, neighbours in Funchal, are discussing the proposed expansion of the cruise terminal and its impact on the island.

lines.speaker: Ana
lines.text: Então, Carlos, o que achas da expansão do terminal de cruzeiros? Parece-me que vai trazer imensos benefícios para a ilha.
lines.translation: So, Carlos, what do you think of the cruise terminal expansion? It seems to me it will bring immense benefits to the island.

lines.speaker: Carlos
lines.text: Pois, Ana, benefícios... Não tenho a certeza. Já temos turistas a mais em certas alturas, e isto pode ser prejudicial para os locais.
lines.translation: Well, Ana, benefits... I'm not sure. We already have too many tourists at certain times, and this could be detrimental to locals.

lines.speaker: Ana
lines.text: Prejudicial? Mas pensa no emprego, no comércio! É um investimento enorme que gera riqueza. A meu ver, é essencial para a nossa economia.
lines.translation: Detrimental? But think about jobs, about trade! It's a huge investment that generates wealth. In my view, it's essential for our economy.

lines.speaker: Carlos
lines.text: Concordo que cria empregos, sim, mas a que custo? Os preços das casas sobem, as ruas ficam cheias. A qualidade de vida dos residentes pode piorar, não achas?
lines.translation: I agree it creates jobs, yes, but at what cost? House prices go up, the streets get crowded. Residents' quality of life could worsen, don't you think?

lines.speaker: Ana
lines.text: Percebo o teu ponto de vista sobre os preços, é uma preocupação válida. No entanto, com um bom planeamento, podemos gerir isso. Não podemos fechar a porta ao progresso.
lines.translation: I understand your point of view about prices, it's a valid concern. However, with good planning, we can manage that. We can't close the door to progress.

lines.speaker: Carlos
lines.text: Progresso, sim, mas com cabeça. Ainda assim, penso que devíamos focar-nos mais em diversificar a economia, não depender tanto só do turismo. É uma perspetiva que defendo há anos.
lines.translation: Progress, yes, but with thought. Even so, I think we should focus more on diversifying the economy, not depending so much only on tourism. It's a perspective I've defended for years.

lines.speaker: Ana
lines.text: Admito que diversificar é importante. Mas o turismo já é uma realidade forte. Acho que temos de otimizar o que já temos e melhorar as infraestruturas. É a minha opinião.
lines.translation: I admit that diversifying is important. But tourism is already a strong reality. I think we have to optimize what we already have and improve the infrastructure. That's my opinion.

lines.speaker: Carlos
lines.text: Pois, Ana, vamos ver. Espero que tenhas razão e que não nos arrependamos daqui a uns anos.
lines.translation: Well, Ana, we'll see. I hope you're right and we don't regret it in a few years.

cultural_notes.title: Expressing Nuance in Madeiran Discussions
cultural_notes.body: In Madeira, as in mainland Portugal, discussions can be lively and passionate, but it's often valued to express nuance rather than taking extreme stances. Phrases like 'Concordo em parte,' 'Até percebo o seu ponto de vista,' or 'No entanto...' are common to show you've considered the other person's argument even if you ultimately disagree. Direct, aggressive confrontation is generally avoided in casual settings; instead, people tend to 'hold their ground' with polite but firm counter-arguments.

cultural_notes.title: The Role of 'Pois' and 'Pois é'
cultural_notes.body: The words 'Pois' or 'Pois é' are incredibly versatile in Portuguese. In a debate, they can indicate partial agreement ('Pois, é verdade'), reluctant concession ('Pois, tem razão'), or even a 'let's agree to disagree' sentiment ('Pois, vamos ver'). They often serve as a conversational filler that acknowledges what the other person said without necessarily endorsing it fully, allowing for a smoother transition to your own counter-argument.

roleplay.scenario: You're at your local 'café' in a Madeiran neighbourhood, talking to a neighbour, D.ª Emília, about the controversial proposal to build a new large hotel near a natural reserve.

nodes.npc_text: Bom dia! Então, já soube daquela ideia de construir um hotel enorme perto da Reserva Natural? Na minha opinião, é um disparate completo, vai estragar tudo!
nodes.npc_translation: Good morning! So, have you heard about that idea to build a huge hotel near the Natural Reserve? In my opinion, it's complete nonsense, it will ruin everything!

options.text: Concordo plenamente, D.ª Emília. Aquela zona devia ser protegida.
options.translation: I fully agree, D.ª Emília. That area should be protected.
options.feedback: You've clearly expressed agreement and reinforced her point. Good start!

options.text: Não tenho a certeza se concordo. Talvez traga benefícios para a economia local?
options.translation: I'm not sure if I agree. Maybe it will bring benefits to the local economy?
options.feedback: You've politely expressed disagreement and offered a potential counter-argument. Well done for challenging her view respectfully.

options.text: Não estou a par. O que é que dizem sobre o projeto?
options.translation: I'm not aware. What are they saying about the project?
options.feedback: Asking for more information is a good way to gather context before forming your opinion. Neutral and effective.

nodes.npc_text: Pois é, não sou só eu que penso assim! É uma vergonha. Aquela zona é tão bonita, devia ser protegida, não achas?
nodes.npc_translation: That's right, I'm not the only one who thinks so! It's a shame. That area is so beautiful, it should be protected, don't you think?

options.text: Exatamente! E os impactos ambientais seriam enormes, sem dúvida.
options.translation: Exactly! And the environmental impacts would be huge, no doubt.
options.feedback: You've reinforced your agreement with a strong argument. This leads to a shared conclusion.

options.text: Sim, mas ao mesmo tempo, o turismo é importante para a ilha. Não há um meio-termo?
options.translation: Yes, but at the same time, tourism is important for the island. Isn't there a middle ground?
options.feedback: You've introduced nuance by acknowledging the other side while still holding your ground on protection. Good for L5!

nodes.npc_text: Ah, não concorda? Mas como é que pode ser bom? Acha que não vai ter impacto no ambiente? É que eu não consigo ver os benefícios.
nodes.npc_translation: Oh, you don't agree? But how can it be good? Do you think it won't have an impact on the environment? Because I can't see the benefits.

options.text: Percebo a sua preocupação, mas o hotel criaria muitos postos de trabalho para os locais.
options.translation: I understand your concern, but the hotel would create many jobs for locals.
options.feedback: You've acknowledged her concern before presenting your counter-argument, showing respect while defending your view.

options.text: Ainda assim, penso que com um bom planeamento, os impactos podem ser minimizados.
options.translation: Even so, I think that with good planning, the impacts can be minimized.
options.feedback: You're holding your ground and offering a solution. This shows confidence in your opinion.

nodes.npc_text: Ora, dizem que é para o turismo, para criar empregos. Mas a que preço? É a única reserva que temos ali perto, e já está cheia de gente. Que mais quer saber?
nodes.npc_translation: Well, they say it's for tourism, to create jobs. But at what cost? It's the only reserve we have nearby, and it's already full of people. What else do you want to know?

options.text: Na minha opinião, a proteção ambiental devia vir primeiro que os lucros.
options.translation: In my opinion, environmental protection should come before profits.
options.feedback: You've now clearly stated your opinion, aligning with D.ª Emília's initial stance.

options.text: Compreendo, mas os empregos são importantes. Talvez haja um equilíbrio possível?
options.translation: I understand, but jobs are important. Maybe there's a possible balance?
options.feedback: You're trying to find a middle ground, acknowledging both sides of the argument. This shows good negotiation skills.

nodes.npc_text: Hum... Percebo o seu ponto de vista, que é preciso um equilíbrio. Mas é difícil encontrar esse equilíbrio quando se fala em lucros tão grandes. Não tenho a certeza que seja possível.
nodes.npc_translation: Hmm... I understand your point of view, that a balance is needed. But it's difficult to find that balance when talking about such large profits. I'm not sure it's possible.

options.text: Admito que é um desafio, mas é essencial tentar. Não podemos desistir de proteger a ilha.
options.translation: I admit it's a challenge, but it's essential to try. We can't give up on protecting the island.
options.feedback: You've acknowledged the difficulty but firmly reiterated your core value. This is a good way to conclude with a shared sentiment.

options.text: Pois, vamos ver o que acontece. Cada um tem a sua perspetiva, não é?
options.translation: Well, we'll see what happens. Everyone has their own perspective, don't they?
options.feedback: You've chosen to agree to disagree, acknowledging different perspectives. A common and polite way to end a debate.

nodes.npc_text: Pois, no fundo, acho que estamos de acordo que é uma situação complicada. Espero que as autoridades pensem bem nisto.
nodes.npc_translation: Well, deep down, I think we agree that it's a complicated situation. I hope the authorities think carefully about this.

nodes.npc_text: Bem, cada um tem a sua opinião, não é? Vamos ver o que acontece. Mas eu não vou mudar a minha ideia!
nodes.npc_translation: Well, everyone has their own opinion, don't they? We'll see what happens. But I'm not changing my mind!

dialogues.title: A Chat at the Café
dialogues.context: You're having a coffee with a local acquaintance, discussing daily life in Funchal.

lines.speaker: Marta
lines.text: Então, como é que tem corrido a tua estadia cá na Madeira? Já te sentes mesmo em casa?
lines.translation: So, how has your stay here in Madeira been going? Do you really feel at home yet?

lines.speaker: Learner
lines.text: Sim, sinto-me muito bem, Marta. Adoro a ilha e as pessoas. Há sempre algo novo para descobrir.
lines.translation: Yes, I feel very good, Marta. I love the island and the people. There's always something new to discover.

lines.speaker: Marta
lines.text: Pois é, a Madeira é especial. Mas olha, o que é que achas do aumento do turismo? Achas que é bom para a ilha?
lines.translation: That's right, Madeira is special. But look, what do you think about the increase in tourism? Do you think it's good for the island?

lines.speaker: Learner
lines.text: Há sempre prós e contras, não é verdade? Por um lado, traz imensa receita, mas por outro, o Funchal está cada vez mais cheio.
lines.translation: There are always pros and cons, aren't there? On one hand, it brings a lot of revenue, but on the other, Funchal is getting more and more crowded.

lines.speaker: Marta
lines.text: Exatamente! É um bocado complicado gerir tudo isso. Mas pronto, faz parte.
lines.translation: Exactly! It's a bit complicated to manage all that. But well, it's part of it.

cultural_notes.title: Nuance and Indirectness in Conversation
cultural_notes.body: At L5, conversations often involve discussing topics with multiple facets, like the pros and cons of tourism. Portuguese speakers, especially in informal settings, may express opinions with a degree of nuance or indirectness rather than stark, direct statements. Phrases like 'Há sempre prós e contras' (There are always pros and cons) or 'É um bocado complicado' (It's a bit complicated) allow for a balanced perspective without sounding overly critical or confrontational. Agreement is often signaled with 'Pois é' or 'Exatamente'.

cultural_notes.title: The Role of 'Tu' vs. 'O Senhor/A Senhora'
cultural_notes.body: In Madeira and Portugal, the choice between informal 'tu' and formal 'o senhor/a senhora' is crucial. With peers, friends, or younger people, 'tu' is standard. However, when addressing someone older, in a service context, or in a more formal professional setting, 'o senhor/a senhora' is preferred. Using 'você' with strangers or elders can sound distancing or even impolite. This situation uses 'tu' as it's an informal acquaintance, but the roleplay might introduce a slightly more formal interaction.

roleplay.scenario: You are at a local market in Funchal, buying some fruit. The vendor, an older woman, starts a conversation with you about how things have changed on the island.

nodes.npc_text: Bom dia! Que frutas bonitas que escolheu. Já cá vive há muito tempo, não é? Como é que vê a Madeira a mudar com tanta gente nova e o turismo?
nodes.npc_translation: Good morning! What beautiful fruits you've chosen. You've been living here for a long time, haven't you? How do you see Madeira changing with so many new people and tourism?

options.text: Bom dia! Sim, já estou cá há uns anos. Acho que o turismo traz muita coisa boa, mas também desafios.
options.translation: Good morning! Yes, I've been here for a few years. I think tourism brings a lot of good things, but also challenges.
options.feedback: Excellent, a balanced and polite response. You acknowledge both sides.

options.text: Bom dia! Pois, a ilha está diferente. Para ser sincera, sinto falta da calma de antigamente.
options.translation: Good morning! Well, the island is different. To be honest, I miss the calm of the old days.
options.feedback: Good, you're expressing an honest opinion. Be prepared for the NPC to elaborate on that sentiment.

options.text: Ah, bom dia! A Madeira está sempre a melhorar, não é? É bom ver o progresso.
options.translation: Ah, good morning! Madeira is always improving, isn't it? It's good to see the progress.
options.feedback: A positive and slightly less nuanced response. The NPC might probe further.

nodes.npc_text: Pois é, desafios. Por exemplo, os preços das casas, estão um absurdo! E o trânsito no Funchal, nem se fala. Mas pronto, os turistas também compram as minhas frutas, não é?
nodes.npc_translation: That's right, challenges. For example, house prices, they're absurd! And the traffic in Funchal, don't even get me started. But well, tourists also buy my fruits, don't they?

options.text: É verdade, os preços são um problema. Mas talvez haja formas de gerir melhor o desenvolvimento.
options.translation: It's true, prices are a problem. But perhaps there are ways to better manage development.
options.feedback: Very good, you're offering a constructive thought without sounding preachy.

options.text: Sim, é uma questão complicada. Mas a beleza da ilha continua a ser única.
options.translation: Yes, it's a complicated issue. But the island's beauty remains unique.
options.feedback: A good way to acknowledge the problem while shifting to a positive aspect.

nodes.npc_text: Ah, sim, a calma... Lembro-me de quando o Funchal era mais sossegado. Agora é um corropio! Mas pronto, também há mais coisas para fazer, mais restaurantes...
nodes.npc_translation: Ah, yes, the calm... I remember when Funchal was quieter. Now it's a hustle and bustle! But well, there are also more things to do, more restaurants...

options.text: É um equilíbrio difícil, entre o progresso e a tradição. O que é que a senhora mais valoriza na Madeira de hoje?
options.translation: It's a difficult balance between progress and tradition. What do you value most about Madeira today?
options.feedback: Excellent, you're asking an open-ended question that invites the NPC to share more personal thoughts.

options.text: Pois é, há mais vida, mas também mais confusão. Mas os mercados como este continuam a ter o seu encanto.
options.translation: That's right, there's more life, but also more confusion. But markets like this still have their charm.
options.feedback: Good, you're acknowledging her point and bringing it back to the immediate context.

nodes.npc_text: Progresso, sim. Mas às vezes parece que esquecem as nossas raízes. Os jovens já não falam a sério sobre as levadas, é só telemóveis e internet. Mas pronto, é o mundo moderno, não é?
nodes.npc_translation: Progress, yes. But sometimes it seems they forget our roots. Young people don't talk seriously about the levadas anymore, it's just mobile phones and the internet. But well, it's the modern world, isn't it?

options.text: É verdade que as tradições são importantes. Mas talvez as novas gerações encontrem novas formas de as manter vivas?
options.translation: It's true that traditions are important. But perhaps new generations will find new ways to keep them alive?
options.feedback: A thoughtful and optimistic response that encourages further discussion.

options.text: Sim, os tempos mudam. Mas as levadas e a natureza da Madeira continuam a ser um grande atrativo, mesmo para os mais novos.
options.translation: Yes, times change. But the levadas and Madeira's nature continue to be a big attraction, even for the younger ones.
options.feedback: Good, you're highlighting a persistent positive aspect.

nodes.npc_text: Gerir... Pois, isso é que é o problema. Mas quem é que sabe gerir isto tudo? Os políticos? Ai, ai... Mas enfim, obrigada pela conversa e pelas suas compras. Volte sempre!
nodes.npc_translation: Manage... Well, that's the problem. But who knows how to manage all this? The politicians? Oh dear... But anyway, thank you for the conversation and your purchases. Come back always!

nodes.npc_text: Ah, sim, a beleza da ilha é inegável. É por isso que tanta gente vem cá, não é? E os nossos mercados, cheios de cor! Obrigada e volte sempre!
nodes.npc_translation: Ah, yes, the island's beauty is undeniable. That's why so many people come here, isn't it? And our markets, full of color! Thank you and come back always!

nodes.npc_text: O que mais valorizo? A nossa gente, a nossa forma de ser. Apesar de tudo, somos um povo acolhedor e isso não muda. Obrigada pela conversa, menina. Volte sempre!
nodes.npc_translation: What I value most? Our people, our way of being. Despite everything, we are a welcoming people and that doesn't change. Thank you for the conversation, dear. Come back always!
