dialogues.title: Calling for a Boiler Repair
dialogues.context: A guest house owner is calling a repair service because the boiler isn't working and guests have no hot water.

lines.speaker: Service
lines.text: Bom dia, assistência técnica. Diga!
lines.translation: Good morning, technical assistance. How can I help?

lines.speaker: Host
lines.text: Olá, bom dia. O meu esquentador não está a funcionar. Não há água quente nenhuma.
lines.translation: Hello, good morning. My water heater isn't working. There's no hot water at all.

lines.speaker: Service
lines.text: Pois não. Qual é a sua morada, por favor?
lines.translation: Oh, I see. What's your address, please?

lines.speaker: Host
lines.text: É na Rua do Castanheiro, número quinze. É urgente, tenho hóspedes.
lines.translation: It's on Rua do Castanheiro, number fifteen. It's urgent, I have guests.

lines.speaker: Service
lines.text: Certo. Podemos enviar um técnico hoje à tarde, entre as duas e as quatro. Serve?
lines.translation: Understood. We can send a technician this afternoon, between two and four. Does that work?

lines.speaker: Host
lines.text: Sim, serve perfeitamente. Muito obrigada!
lines.translation: Yes, that works perfectly. Thank you very much!

cultural_notes.title: Addressing Service Professionals
cultural_notes.body: In Portugal, when speaking to service professionals (like technicians, shop assistants, or receptionists), it's common to use 'o senhor' / 'a senhora' or simply use the third person verb conjugation as a polite form of address, rather than 'você'. Starting with 'Olá, bom dia' and ending with 'Muito obrigada' is standard polite practice.

cultural_notes.title: Expressing Urgency
cultural_notes.body: If a situation is time-sensitive, like having guests without hot water, it's appropriate to state 'É urgente' (It's urgent) or explain the reason for the urgency. This helps the service provider understand the priority.

roleplay.scenario: You are calling a local company in Madeira because the boiler in your rental property is not working and there is no hot water for your guests. You need to explain the problem and arrange for a technician to visit.

nodes.npc_text: Bom dia, empresa de assistência técnica. Diga!
nodes.npc_translation: Good morning, technical assistance company. How can I help?

options.text: Olá, bom dia. O meu esquentador não está a funcionar.
options.translation: Hello, good morning. My water heater isn't working.
options.feedback: A good clear start, stating the problem directly.

options.text: Preciso de um técnico para o esquentador.
options.translation: I need a technician for the water heater.
options.feedback: Also good, but slightly less detailed than the first option.

nodes.npc_text: Pois não. E qual é a avaria exatamente? Não tem água quente?
nodes.npc_translation: Oh, I see. And what's the fault exactly? You don't have hot water?

options.text: Exatamente, não há água quente nenhuma.
options.translation: Exactly, there's no hot water at all.
options.feedback: Clear confirmation of the problem.

options.text: Sim, não sai água quente da torneira.
options.translation: Yes, no hot water comes out of the tap.
options.feedback: A good alternative, adding a bit more detail.

nodes.npc_text: Certo. E qual é a sua morada, por favor?
nodes.npc_translation: Understood. And what's your address, please?

options.text: É na Rua das Flores, número vinte e cinco.
options.translation: It's on Rua das Flores, number twenty-five.
options.feedback: Providing the address clearly is key.

options.text: A morada é Rua das Flores, vinte e cinco.
options.translation: The address is Rua das Flores, twenty-five.
options.feedback: A slightly more concise way to give the address.

nodes.npc_text: Obrigado. Temos um técnico disponível hoje à tarde, entre as três e as cinco. Serve?
nodes.npc_translation: Thank you. We have a technician available this afternoon, between three and five. Does that work?

options.text: Sim, serve perfeitamente. Muito obrigada!
options.translation: Yes, that works perfectly. Thank you very much!
options.feedback: Confirming the appointment is a great way to finish the call.

options.text: Hoje à tarde é um bocado difícil. Não pode ser amanhã de manhã?
options.translation: This afternoon is a bit difficult. Can it not be tomorrow morning?
options.feedback: It's good to negotiate if the time doesn't suit, but be polite.

nodes.npc_text: Amanhã de manhã temos um espaço entre as nove e as onze. Está bem?
nodes.npc_translation: Tomorrow morning we have a slot between nine and eleven. Is that okay?

options.text: Sim, está ótimo. Muito obrigada!
options.translation: Yes, that's great. Thank you very much!
options.feedback: Excellent, you've successfully arranged a new time.

phrase_patterns.base: Lamento imenso o {problema} com o barulho.

dialogues.title: Guest Complaint
dialogues.context: A guest at an accommodation in Madeira complains to the host about noise from outside.

lines.speaker: Hóspede
lines.text: Desculpe, mas está um bocado barulhento aqui à noite.
lines.translation: Excuse me, but it's a bit noisy here at night.

lines.speaker: Anfitrião
lines.text: Oh, lamento imenso ouvir isso! De onde é que vem o barulho?
lines.translation: Oh, I'm very sorry to hear that! Where does the noise come from?

lines.speaker: Hóspede
lines.text: Parece que é da rua, ou talvez do café ali ao lado. Música e pessoas a falar alto.
lines.translation: It seems to be from the street, or maybe the café next door. Music and people talking loudly.

lines.speaker: Anfitrião
lines.text: Pois é, às vezes acontece. Vou lá dar um salto, p'ra ver se consigo que façam um bocadinho menos de barulho. P'ra já, peço desculpa pelo incómodo.
lines.translation: Yes, it happens sometimes. I'll pop over there to see if I can get them to make a little less noise. For now, I apologize for the inconvenience.

dialogues.title: Host Speaks to Neighbour
dialogues.context: The host approaches a neighbour (a café owner) to ask them to reduce noise.

lines.speaker: Anfitrião
lines.text: Olá, boa noite! Tudo bem? Desculpe incomodar a esta hora...
lines.translation: Hello, good evening! All well? Sorry to bother you at this hour...

lines.speaker: Dono do Café
lines.text: Olá! Pois é, tudo a correr. Diga lá!
lines.translation: Hello! Yes, everything's going well. Tell me!

lines.speaker: Anfitrião
lines.text: É que tenho uns hóspedes ali ao lado e estão-se a queixar do barulho. Será que podiam fazer um bocado menos de barulho, por favor? Principalmente com a música.
lines.translation: It's just that I have some guests next door and they're complaining about the noise. Could you please make a bit less noise, please? Especially with the music.

lines.speaker: Dono do Café
lines.text: Ah, pois, claro. Desculpe lá. Vamos ter mais atenção, sem problema. Obrigado por avisar.
lines.translation: Ah, yes, of course. Sorry about that. We'll be more careful, no problem. Thanks for letting us know.

cultural_notes.title: Indirectness in Complaints
cultural_notes.body: In Portugal, especially when dealing with neighbours or local businesses, direct complaints are often softened with apologies or indirect phrasing. Starting with 'Desculpe incomodar...' (Sorry to bother you...) or 'É que tenho uns hóspedes...' (It's just that I have some guests...) helps maintain good relations. A direct 'Faça menos barulho!' (Make less noise!) would be considered impolite.

cultural_notes.title: The Role of 'Pois'
cultural_notes.body: 'Pois' and 'Pois é' are very common interjections in European Portuguese. They often mean 'indeed', 'that's right', or 'well'. In conversations, they can signal agreement, understanding, or simply acknowledge what the other person has said before responding. It's a natural filler that adds to the flow of conversation.

roleplay.scenario: You are the host of a small guesthouse in Madeira. A guest approaches you to complain about noise late at night. You need to understand the problem, apologize, and offer a solution.

nodes.npc_text: Bom dia! Desculpe incomodar, mas tive uma noite um bocado difícil com o barulho.
nodes.npc_translation: Good morning! Sorry to bother you, but I had a bit of a difficult night with the noise.

options.text: Oh, lamento imenso! De onde é que vinha o barulho?
options.translation: Oh, I'm so sorry! Where was the noise coming from?
options.feedback: Excellent start! You apologized and asked for details.

options.text: Barulho? Que barulho?
options.translation: Noise? What noise?
options.feedback: It's better to apologize first, even if you don't know the source. This might sound a bit dismissive.

nodes.npc_text: Parece que era da rua, daquele café ali em baixo. Música e pessoas a rir muito alto, até tarde.
nodes.npc_translation: It seems it was from the street, from that café down there. Music and people laughing very loudly, until late.

options.text: Pois é, às vezes acontece. Vou lá dar um salto e peço-lhes para terem mais atenção.
options.translation: Yes, it happens sometimes. I'll pop over there and ask them to be more careful.
options.feedback: Good, you acknowledged the problem and offered a concrete step.

options.text: Não posso fazer nada, é a vida noturna da cidade.
options.translation: I can't do anything, it's city nightlife.
options.feedback: This response is unhelpful and might upset your guest. Try to offer a solution or show empathy.

nodes.npc_text: Muito obrigado! Agradeço imenso a sua ajuda. É que preciso de descansar bem.
nodes.npc_translation: Thank you very much! I really appreciate your help. It's just that I need to rest well.

options.text: Não se preocupe, vou resolver isso. Peço desculpa pelo incómodo.
options.translation: Don't worry, I'll sort it out. I apologize for the inconvenience.
options.feedback: Perfect! You reassured the guest and apologized again.

nodes.npc_text: Muito obrigado! Assim já me sinto mais tranquilo.
nodes.npc_translation: Thank you very much! Now I feel more at ease.

nodes.npc_text: Ah... pois. Entendo. Que pena.
nodes.npc_translation: Ah... well. I understand. What a pity.

cultural_notes.title: Rubbish Collection in Madeira
cultural_notes.body: In Madeira, as in much of Portugal, rubbish collection often follows a specific schedule. It's common for residents to place their household waste in designated street containers (contentores) only on certain days or evenings, rather than daily. Always check the local schedule or with your host to avoid fines or inconvenience.

cultural_notes.title: Directness and Politeness
cultural_notes.body: Portuguese communication is generally direct, but politeness is always appreciated. When handing over keys or explaining rules, a friendly, clear tone is best. Using 'por favor' (please) and 'obrigado/a' (thank you) is essential. For private rentals, 'tu' (informal 'you') is often used between host and guest, especially if the host is younger or wants to establish a friendly rapport.

roleplay.scenario: You are a guest arriving at an apartment in Funchal, Madeira. The host, Senhor Costa, is meeting you to hand over the keys and explain a few things about the flat.

nodes.npc_text: Olá! Bem-vindo ao apartamento. Eu sou o Senhor Costa. Aqui estão as chaves.
nodes.npc_translation: Hello! Welcome to the apartment. I'm Mr. Costa. Here are the keys.

options.text: Muito obrigado, Senhor Costa!
options.translation: Thank you very much, Mr. Costa!
options.feedback: A good polite start. Now you can ask about the Wi-Fi.

options.text: As chaves, obrigado.
options.translation: The keys, thank you.
options.feedback: A bit brief, but acceptable. Try to be a little more enthusiastic next time.

nodes.npc_text: De nada. Então, sobre a internet, o Wi-Fi é 'HóspedesFunchal' e a palavra-passe é 'solmadeira'.
nodes.npc_translation: You're welcome. So, about the internet, the Wi-Fi is 'HóspedesFunchal' and the password is 'solmadeira'.

options.text: Ok, entendi. E o lixo, quando é para pôr?
options.translation: Okay, I understand. And the rubbish, when is it to be put out?
options.feedback: Good, you understood and moved on to the next important question.

options.text: Pode repetir a palavra-passe, por favor?
options.translation: Can you repeat the password, please?
options.feedback: It's fine to ask for repetition if you didn't catch it. Always use 'por favor'.

nodes.npc_text: Claro. A palavra-passe é 'solmadeira', tudo em minúsculas.
nodes.npc_translation: Of course. The password is 'solmadeira', all lowercase.

options.text: Obrigado! Agora, o lixo. Quando é que é para pôr?
options.translation: Thank you! Now, the rubbish. When is it to be put out?
options.feedback: Excellent, you got the information and moved on.

nodes.npc_text: O lixo é para pôr no contentor cá fora, na rua, às segundas e quintas-feiras à noite.
nodes.npc_translation: The rubbish is to be put in the bin outside, on the street, on Monday and Thursday evenings.

options.text: Certo. E se tiver uma emergência?
options.translation: Understood. And if I have an emergency?
options.feedback: Good question, important to know about emergencies.

options.text: Ok. Mais alguma coisa importante?
options.translation: Okay. Anything else important?
options.feedback: A good general question. The host will likely cover emergencies next.

nodes.npc_text: Em caso de emergência, ligue para o 112, ou para o meu telemóvel. O número está no frigorífico.
nodes.npc_translation: In case of emergency, call 112, or my mobile. The number is on the fridge.

options.text: Muito obrigado por toda a informação, Senhor Costa! Tenha um bom dia.
options.translation: Thank you very much for all the information, Mr. Costa! Have a good day.
options.feedback: Perfect closing! You've covered all the main points politely.

cultural_notes.title: Addressing Service Professionals
cultural_notes.body: In Portugal, it's common and polite to address cleaners, shopkeepers, or other service providers, especially older women, with 'Dona' (D.) followed by their first name (e.g., 'D. Maria'). Alternatively, 'a senhora' (the lady) is a respectful way to refer to them or address them directly.

cultural_notes.title: Clarity in Instructions
cultural_notes.body: When giving instructions, especially for tasks like cleaning, be clear and specific. Portuguese people appreciate directness in these situations. Use phrases like 'Preciso que...' (I need you to...) or 'É preciso...' (It's necessary to...) followed by the action.

roleplay.scenario: You are briefing D. Maria, the cleaner, about what needs to be done for the upcoming guest check-in.

nodes.npc_text: Bom dia! Então, o que é que é preciso fazer hoje?
nodes.npc_translation: Good morning! So, what needs to be done today?

options.text: Bom dia, D. Maria. Preciso que limpe o apartamento todo.

options.feedback: Excellent, a clear and polite instruction.

options.text: Olá! Pode limpar a casa para o check-in de amanhã?

options.feedback: Good, you've stated the main task and the reason.

nodes.npc_text: Claro. E alguma coisa em especial?

options.text: Sim, é importante repor o sabonete e o papel higiénico.
options.translation: Yes, it's important to restock the soap and toilet paper.
options.feedback: Well done, specific items for restocking.

options.text: Sim, por favor, reponha os produtos nas casas de banho.
options.translation: Yes, please, restock the products in the bathrooms.
options.feedback: Good, a general instruction for bathroom products.

nodes.npc_text: Entendido. E a roupa da cama e as toalhas, onde estão?

options.translation: They are in the laundry basket, in the main bathroom.

options.feedback: Clear and polite, specifying the time.

options.text: O check-in é só à tarde, então pode vir de manhã.

options.feedback: Excellent, providing context for the timing.

nodes.npc_text: Combinado! Então, até amanhã de manhã.
nodes.npc_translation: Agreed! So, see you tomorrow morning.

lines.text: É na casa de banho, debaixo do lavatório. Está a pingar um bocado, sim, é urgente.
lines.translation: It's in the bathroom, under the sink. It's dripping quite a bit, yes, it's urgent.

lines.speaker: Plumber (Phone)
lines.text: Percebo. Consigo ir aí hoje à tarde, p'ra aí às três. Serve?
lines.translation: I understand. I can go there this afternoon, around three. Does that work?

lines.speaker: You
lines.text: Ótimo! E quanto é a visita? Já inclui o arranjo?
lines.translation: Great! And how much is the visit? Does it already include the repair?

lines.speaker: Plumber (Phone)
lines.text: A visita são 25 euros, p'ra ver o problema. Depois o arranjo é à parte, dou-lhe um orçamento.
lines.translation: The visit is 25 euros, to see the problem. Then the repair is separate, I'll give you a quote.

lines.speaker: You
lines.text: Pois, está bem. Então, até às três!
lines.translation: Right, that's fine. So, see you at three!

cultural_notes.title: Professional Courtesy and Pricing
cultural_notes.body: When calling a service professional in Portugal, it's common to be direct but polite. Always address them as 'o senhor' or 'a senhora' unless they explicitly invite you to use 'tu'. It's also standard practice to ask about the cost of the visit ('quanto custa a visita?') and whether it includes the repair or if you'll receive a separate quote ('orçamento') for the work itself. Don't be shy about asking for this information upfront.

cultural_notes.title: Indicating Urgency
cultural_notes.body: To convey urgency, you can use phrases like 'é urgente' (it's urgent) or 'está a pingar bastante' (it's dripping a lot). Madeirans often use 'um bocado' (a bit) in conversation, even to emphasize, as in 'preocupado um bocado' (quite worried), which adds a natural, less dramatic tone than 'muito' (very) in some contexts.

roleplay.scenario: You are calling a plumber in Madeira to report a water leak. You need to explain where it is, how urgent it is, and ask about the cost and availability.

nodes.npc_text: Bom dia, Canalizações Funchal, diga!
nodes.npc_translation: Good morning, Funchal Plumbing, speak!

options.text: Bom dia. Tenho uma fuga de água em casa.
options.translation: Good morning. I have a water leak at home.
options.feedback: Good start! Clear and direct.

options.text: Olá, preciso de um canalizador, tenho um problema com água.
options.translation: Hello, I need a plumber, I have a problem with water.
options.feedback: You're getting there, but 'fuga de água' is more precise.

nodes.npc_text: Pois, senhora/senhor. Onde é a fuga? É urgente?
nodes.npc_translation: Right, madam/sir. Where is the leak? Is it urgent?

options.text: É na cozinha, debaixo da banca. Está a pingar bastante, sim, é urgente.
options.translation: It's in the kitchen, under the counter. It's dripping quite a lot, yes, it's urgent.
options.feedback: Excellent! You've located it and conveyed urgency.

options.text: É na casa de banho. Não sei se é urgente.
options.translation: It's in the bathroom. I don't know if it's urgent.
options.feedback: Good, but try to give more detail about the urgency next time. 'Está a pingar muito' (it's dripping a lot) helps.

nodes.npc_text: Percebo. Consigo ir aí hoje à tarde, p'ra aí às quatro. Serve?
nodes.npc_translation: I understand. I can go there this afternoon, around four. Does that work?

options.text: Sim, perfeito! Quatro horas está ótimo. E quanto custa a visita?
options.translation: Yes, perfect! Four o'clock is great. And how much does the visit cost?
options.feedback: Perfect! You confirmed the time and moved on to the next important question.

options.text: Hoje à tarde é um bocado difícil. Consegue vir amanhã de manhã?
options.translation: This afternoon is a bit difficult. Can you come tomorrow morning?
options.feedback: Good job negotiating the time. Now, remember to ask about the price.

nodes.npc_text: Amanhã de manhã consigo, p'ra aí às dez. E quanto à visita, são 25 euros.
nodes.npc_translation: Tomorrow morning I can, around ten. And regarding the visit, it's 25 euros.

options.text: Ótimo, então amanhã às dez. O arranjo é à parte, certo?
options.translation: Great, so tomorrow at ten. The repair is separate, right?
options.feedback: Good confirmation and clarification about the repair cost.

nodes.npc_text: A visita são 25 euros, para ver o problema e dar um orçamento. O arranjo é à parte.
nodes.npc_translation: The visit is 25 euros, to see the problem and give a quote. The repair is separate.

options.text: Está bem, percebo. Então ficamos para as quatro. Muito obrigada!
options.translation: Okay, I understand. So, we'll keep it for four o'clock. Thank you very much!
options.feedback: Excellent! You've successfully arranged the visit and understood the pricing. Conversation complete.

options.text: Pois, está bem. E o orçamento é gratuito?
options.translation: Right, okay. And is the quote free?
options.feedback: Good question for clarification, but the plumber already mentioned it's for 'ver o problema e dar um orçamento'. You've confirmed the price; it's time to conclude.

dialogues.title: Initial Call to Finanças
dialogues.context: You're calling the Finanças (tax office) in Madeira because you have an issue with your NIF.

lines.speaker: Atendimento Finanças
lines.text: Autoridade Tributária e Aduaneira, boa tarde. Em que posso ajudar?
lines.translation: Tax and Customs Authority, good afternoon. How can I help?

lines.speaker: Você
lines.text: Boa tarde. Ligo sobre o meu NIF. Está inativo e preciso de o ativar.
lines.translation: Good afternoon. I'm calling about my NIF. It's inactive and I need to activate it.

lines.speaker: Atendimento Finanças
lines.text: Pois, entendo. Para ativação de NIF, terá mesmo de se dirigir a um balcão de atendimento. Não conseguimos fazer isso por telefone.
lines.translation: Right, I understand. For NIF activation, you'll really have to go to a service desk. We can't do that over the phone.

lines.speaker: Você
lines.text: Ah, pois. E que documentos preciso de levar?
lines.translation: Ah, right. And what documents do I need to bring?

lines.speaker: Atendimento Finanças
lines.text: Precisa do seu Cartão de Cidadão ou passaporte, e um comprovativo de morada recente. E, se for o caso, a declaração de início de atividade.
lines.translation: You need your Citizen Card or passport, and a recent proof of address. And, if applicable, your business registration statement.

lines.speaker: Você
lines.text: Certo. Muito obrigado pela informação.
lines.translation: Okay. Thank you very much for the information.

dialogues.title: Clarifying a Tax Declaration Issue
dialogues.context: You're calling Finanças again, this time about a problem with your tax declaration.

lines.speaker: Atendimento Finanças
lines.text: Finanças, diga.
lines.translation: Finanças, speak.

lines.speaker: Você
lines.text: Boa tarde. Tenho um problema com a minha declaração de IRS do ano passado. Acho que há um erro.
lines.translation: Good afternoon. I have a problem with my IRS declaration from last year. I think there's an error.

lines.speaker: Atendimento Finanças
lines.text: Pois, para questões de retificação de declarações, é algo que normalmente se resolve online no Portal das Finanças. Já tentou por lá?
lines.translation: Right, for declaration rectification issues, it's something usually resolved online on the Finanças Portal. Have you tried there?

lines.speaker: Você
lines.text: Já tentei, mas não consigo. É um bocado complicado p'ra mim. Será que posso agendar um atendimento presencial?
lines.translation: I've tried, but I can't. It's a bit complicated for me. Can I schedule an in-person appointment?

lines.speaker: Atendimento Finanças
lines.text: Sim, claro. Tem de aceder ao Portal das Finanças e procurar por 'Agendamento'. Lá pode escolher o balcão e a hora que lhe der mais jeito.
lines.translation: Yes, of course. You have to access the Finanças Portal and look for 'Appointment'. There you can choose the office and time that suits you best.

lines.speaker: Você
lines.text: Ah, muito bem. Então vou tentar fazer isso. Obrigada.
lines.translation: Ah, very good. So I'll try to do that. Thank you.

cultural_notes.title: Patience with Bureaucracy
cultural_notes.body: Dealing with government offices (like Finanças) in Portugal often requires patience. Phone lines can be busy, and some issues require an in-person visit. Always be prepared to wait a bit or be redirected. It's common to be told to resolve issues online first.

cultural_notes.title: The Importance of NIF
cultural_notes.body: The NIF (Número de Identificação Fiscal) is your tax identification number and is absolutely crucial for almost all transactions in Portugal, from opening a bank account to buying groceries (if you want to add it to a receipt for tax purposes). Keep it handy and know its status.

roleplay.scenario: You are calling the Finanças office in Funchal, Madeira, because your NIF seems to be inactive or incorrect, and you cannot use it for an important transaction. You need to understand how to fix it and what documents to bring.

nodes.npc_text: Autoridade Tributária e Aduaneira, boa tarde. Em que posso ajudar?
nodes.npc_translation: Tax and Customs Authority, good afternoon. How can I help?

options.text: Boa tarde. Ligo sobre um problema com o meu NIF.
options.translation: Good afternoon. I'm calling about a problem with my NIF.
options.feedback: A good, direct start. 'Ligo sobre' is perfect for stating the reason for your call.

options.text: Diga. O meu NIF não está a funcionar.
options.translation: Speak. My NIF isn't working.
options.feedback: A bit informal with 'Diga' for a first contact with a service, but acceptable. Make sure to be polite next time.

nodes.npc_text: Pois não. Qual é o problema específico com o seu NIF?
nodes.npc_translation: Right. What is the specific problem with your NIF?

options.text: Parece que está inativo. Não consigo utilizá-lo para nada.
options.translation: It seems to be inactive. I can't use it for anything.
options.feedback: Clearly stating the problem helps the agent direct you correctly.

options.text: Não sei, está incorreto ou algo do género.
options.translation: I don't know, it's incorrect or something like that.
options.feedback: Try to be more specific if you can, but this is understandable. The agent will likely ask for more details.

nodes.npc_text: Compreendo. Para resolver problemas de NIF inativo ou incorreto, terá de se dirigir pessoalmente a um balcão de atendimento. Não é possível fazer isso por telefone.
nodes.npc_translation: I understand. To resolve issues with an inactive or incorrect NIF, you will need to go in person to a service desk. It's not possible to do that over the phone.

options.text: Ah, pois. E que documentos preciso de levar comigo?
options.translation: Ah, right. And what documents do I need to bring with me?
options.feedback: Excellent follow-up question. Always ask what to bring before going to a government office.

options.text: Certo. Tenho de ir lá. Obrigada.
options.translation: Okay. I have to go there. Thank you.
options.feedback: This ends the call, but you missed an opportunity to ask about the required documents. You'll need to call again or risk a wasted trip.

nodes.npc_text: Para ativar ou corrigir o NIF, precisará do seu Cartão de Cidadão ou passaporte, e um comprovativo de morada recente. Leve os originais, por favor.
nodes.npc_translation: To activate or correct your NIF, you will need your Citizen Card or passport, and a recent proof of address. Please bring the originals.

options.text: Certo. Cartão de Cidadão e comprovativo de morada. Muito obrigada pela ajuda.
options.translation: Okay. Citizen Card and proof of address. Thank you very much for your help.
options.feedback: Confirming the information is a good way to ensure you understood correctly.

options.text: Só o Cartão de Cidadão? Não preciso de mais nada?
options.translation: Only the Citizen Card? I don't need anything else?
options.feedback: It's good to clarify, but the agent did mention 'comprovativo de morada'. Listen carefully.

nodes.npc_text: Não, como referi, precisa do Cartão de Cidadão E um comprovativo de morada. São os dois documentos essenciais. E os originais, sim.
nodes.npc_translation: No, as I mentioned, you need your Citizen Card AND a proof of address. Those are the two essential documents. And the originals, yes.

options.text: Peço desculpa, entendi mal. Muito obrigada por esclarecer.
options.translation: I apologize, I misunderstood. Thank you very much for clarifying.
options.feedback: Apologizing when you've misunderstood is polite and appropriate.

nodes.npc_text: De nada. Tenha um bom dia.
nodes.npc_translation: You're welcome. Have a good day.

cultural_notes.title: The 'Centro de Saúde' in Portugal
cultural_notes.body: The 'centro de saúde' is the primary point of contact for public healthcare in Portugal, including Madeira. It's where you register to access the national health service (SNS), get a family doctor, and have routine appointments. It's not typically for emergencies, which go to hospitals.

cultural_notes.title: The 'Médico de Família'
cultural_notes.body: Having a 'médico de família' (family doctor) is central to the Portuguese public health system. They manage your general health, refer you to specialists, and prescribe medication. There can sometimes be a waiting list to be assigned one, especially in more populated areas.

roleplay.scenario: You are at the reception desk of a health centre in Funchal, Madeira. You need to register and ask about getting a family doctor.

nodes.npc_text: Bom dia. Em que posso ajudar?
nodes.npc_translation: Good morning. How can I help?

options.text: Bom dia. Queria registar-me aqui no centro de saúde.
options.translation: Good morning. I'd like to register here at the health centre.
options.feedback: Excellent start! Clear and polite.

options.text: Preciso de me inscrever para ter um médico.
options.translation: I need to sign up to have a doctor.
options.feedback: Good, but 'registar-me' is more common for initial registration. 'Inscrever-me' is also understood.

nodes.npc_text: Certo. Tem o seu cartão de cidadão e um comprovativo de morada consigo?
nodes.npc_translation: Alright. Do you have your citizen card and proof of address with you?

options.text: Sim, tenho tudo aqui. E também preciso de um médico de família.
options.translation: Yes, I have everything here. And I also need a family doctor.
options.feedback: Perfect! You provided the info and added your next request naturally.

options.text: Tenho o cartão de cidadão, mas não sei o que é um comprovativo de morada.
options.translation: I have my citizen card, but I don't know what a proof of address is.
options.feedback: Good, it's okay to ask for clarification. You're using 'não sei' correctly.

nodes.npc_text: É um documento que prova onde mora, como uma conta da água ou da eletricidade. Tem alguma dessas?
nodes.npc_translation: It's a document that proves where you live, like a water or electricity bill. Do you have one of those?

options.text: Ah, sim! Tenho a conta da luz. Está aqui.
options.translation: Oh, yes! I have the electricity bill. It's here.
options.feedback: Great, you understood and confirmed. 'Conta da luz' is a good example.

nodes.npc_text: Muito bem. Então, preencha, por favor, este formulário. Depois vemos a questão do médico de família.
nodes.npc_translation: Very good. So, please fill out this form. Afterwards, we'll look into the family doctor matter.

options.text: Pois, claro. Muito obrigado.
options.translation: Yes, of course. Thank you very much.
options.feedback: A polite and natural way to end the interaction for now. 'Pois, claro' is typically Portuguese.

options.text: E quanto tempo demora para ter o médico?
options.translation: And how long does it take to get the doctor?
options.feedback: A good follow-up question. You're asking about the process.

nodes.npc_text: Isso pode demorar um bocado, depende da disponibilidade. Mas assim que tiver o registo feito, já pode marcar consultas com qualquer médico de serviço.
nodes.npc_translation: That can take a while, it depends on availability. But once you're registered, you can already book appointments with any on-duty doctor.

options.text: Ah, está bem. Muito obrigado pela informação.
options.translation: Ah, okay. Thank you very much for the information.
options.feedback: You acknowledged the information and thanked them politely. A good, natural ending.

dialogues.context: A client is at a bank branch in Funchal, asking to open a new account and about the process.

lines.text: Bom dia! Em que posso ajudar?

lines.speaker: Cliente
lines.text: Bom dia. Queria abrir uma conta, por favor.

cultural_notes.title: Required Documents for Bank Accounts in Portugal
cultural_notes.body: To open a bank account in Portugal, you typically need several documents: an identification document (passport or Portuguese ID card), a NIF (Número de Identificação Fiscal - tax identification number), proof of address (comprovativo de morada, e.g., a utility bill or residence certificate), and proof of income (comprovativo de rendimentos, e.g., a payslip or employment contract).

cultural_notes.title: The Multibanco Network
cultural_notes.body: Multibanco is Portugal's interbank network, offering a wide range of services beyond just cash withdrawals. You can pay bills, top up mobile phones, buy concert tickets, and even pay taxes at any Multibanco ATM. It's a very convenient and widely used system across the country, including Madeira.

roleplay.scenario: You are at a bank counter in Funchal, trying to open a new account. The bank clerk is guiding you through the process.

nodes.npc_text: Bom dia! Em que posso ajudar?
nodes.npc_translation: Good morning! How can I help?

options.text: Queria abrir uma conta bancária, por favor.
options.translation: I'd like to open a bank account, please.
options.feedback: Excellent, direct and polite.

options.text: Preciso de informações sobre como abrir uma conta.
options.translation: I need information on how to open an account.
options.feedback: A good way to start if you're unsure, leading to the same information.

nodes.npc_text: Claro. Para abrir uma conta, preciso do seu documento de identificação, NIF, comprovativo de morada e comprovativo de rendimentos.
nodes.npc_translation: Of course. To open an account, I need your identification document, NIF, proof of address, and proof of income.

options.text: Tenho tudo comigo.
options.translation: I have everything with me.
options.feedback: Great, you're prepared!

options.text: O que é um comprovativo de morada?
options.translation: What is proof of address?
options.feedback: Good question to clarify an important document.

nodes.npc_text: Um comprovativo de morada pode ser uma fatura da água, luz ou telefone em seu nome, ou um atestado de residência da Junta de Freguesia.
nodes.npc_translation: Proof of address can be a water, electricity, or phone bill in your name, or a residence certificate from the Parish Council.

options.text: Ah, sim, tenho uma fatura da luz.
options.translation: Ah, yes, I have an electricity bill.
options.feedback: Perfect, you've identified the correct document.

options.text: Não tenho nenhum desses agora.
options.translation: I don't have any of those right now.
options.feedback: It's important to be honest, but this will halt the process for now.

nodes.npc_text: Ótimo. Agora, quanto ao cartão, prefere um de débito ou de crédito? E quer acesso ao homebanking e à app?
nodes.npc_translation: Great. Now, regarding the card, do you prefer debit or credit? And do you want access to homebanking and the app?

options.text: Um cartão de débito, por favor, e sim, acesso à app e ao homebanking.
options.translation: A debit card, please, and yes, access to the app and homebanking.
options.feedback: You've clearly stated your preferences.

options.text: Só um cartão de débito, por enquanto.
options.translation: Just a debit card, for now.
options.feedback: A valid choice if you want to keep it simple.

nodes.npc_text: Perfeito. Vamos preencher os formulários, e depois o cartão será enviado para a sua morada. O acesso à app configuramos já.
nodes.npc_translation: Perfect. We'll fill out the forms, and then the card will be sent to your address. We'll set up app access now.

options.text: Muito obrigado/a! Parece simples.
options.translation: Thank you very much! It seems simple.
options.feedback: A polite and appreciative response.

options.text: Quanto tempo demora até o cartão chegar?
options.translation: How long does it take for the card to arrive?
options.feedback: A practical question to manage expectations.

nodes.npc_text: Normalmente, o cartão chega em 5 a 7 dias úteis. Pode levantá-lo aqui ou recebê-lo em casa.
nodes.npc_translation: Normally, the card arrives in 5 to 7 business days. You can pick it up here or receive it at home.

options.text: Receber em casa é melhor. Obrigado/a!
options.translation: Receiving it at home is better. Thank you!
options.feedback: You've made your preference clear.

nodes.npc_text: Sem os documentos completos, não consigo prosseguir. Por favor, volte quando os tiver.
nodes.npc_translation: Without the complete documents, I cannot proceed. Please come back when you have them.

nodes.npc_text: De nada! Se tiver mais alguma questão, diga.
nodes.npc_translation: You're welcome! If you have any more questions, just ask.

dialogues.context: A new resident calls the electricity company (EEM) in Madeira to set up service for their new home.

lines.speaker: Atendente

cultural_notes.title: Addressing Service Staff
cultural_notes.body: In Portugal, when speaking to service staff (e.g., utility companies, shops), it's common and polite to use the third-person singular verb forms (treating them as 'o senhor' or 'a senhora') even if they use 'tu' with you. This shows respect. Phrases like 'Queria ligar...' (I wanted to connect...) are also a polite, indirect way to state your request.

cultural_notes.title: Meter Readings and Payment Methods
cultural_notes.body: When setting up or transferring utility services, you will almost always be asked for the current meter reading ('leitura do contador'). It's a good idea to take a photo of the meter. For bill payment, 'débito direto' (direct debit) is the most common and convenient method for recurring bills. 'Multibanco' refers to the national ATM network and is a common way to pay bills manually using a reference number at an ATM or via online banking.

roleplay.scenario: You are calling the local water company (EMAS) in Funchal to set up water service for your new apartment. You need to provide your address, the meter reading, and choose a payment method.

nodes.npc_text: Pois não. Qual é a morada completa, por favor?

nodes.npc_text: Então, pode verificar e ligar-nos de novo, ou podemos enviar um técnico para fazer a leitura. Prefere qual?
nodes.npc_translation: Then, you can check and call us back, or we can send a technician to take the reading. Which do you prefer?

options.text: Vou verificar e ligo mais tarde.

nodes.npc_text: Obrigada. E como quer pagar a fatura? Prefere débito direto ou multibanco?

options.text: Prefiro débito direto, por favor.

options.text: Multibanco, por favor.

nodes.npc_text: Certo. A ligação da água fica ativa hoje. Obrigada pela sua chamada.
nodes.npc_translation: Right. The water connection will be active today. Thank you for your call.

options.text: Muito obrigada. Bom dia.

dialogues.title: Applying for Residency Certificate
dialogues.context: At the Junta de Freguesia office in Madeira. The learner needs a residency certificate for administrative purposes.

lines.speaker: Atendente
lines.text: Bom dia. Diga, em que posso ajudar?

lines.speaker: Atendente

cultural_notes.title: The Role of the Junta de Freguesia
cultural_notes.body: In Portugal, the Junta de Freguesia (Parish Council) is the local administrative body responsible for many day-to-day citizen services. This includes issuing certificates like 'atestados de residência' (certificates of residency), which are often required for various bureaucratic processes such as applying for a NIF, registering with social security, or enrolling children in school. It's a fundamental point of contact for residents, especially for those new to a parish.

cultural_notes.title: Proof of Address (Comprovativo de Morada)
cultural_notes.body: When asked for 'comprovativo de morada' (proof of address) in Portugal, the most commonly accepted documents are recent utility bills (faturas de água, luz, gás) or bank statements. These documents must be in your name and show your current address. It's always a good idea to have a recent one (less than 3 months old) available when dealing with official matters.

roleplay.scenario: You are at the Junta de Freguesia counter. You need a certificate of residency to enroll your child in a local school. The attendant is asking for details and documents.

nodes.npc_text: Boa tarde. Em que posso ser útil?
nodes.npc_translation: Good afternoon. How can I be of assistance?

options.text: Boa tarde. Eu preciso de um atestado de residência, por favor.
options.translation: Good afternoon. I need a certificate of residency, please.
options.feedback: Excellent start! Direct and polite.

options.text: Olá. Queria um atestado de morada.
options.translation: Hello. I wanted an address certificate.
options.feedback: Good attempt, but 'atestado de residência' is the correct term. 'Atestado de morada' might be understood but isn't standard.

nodes.npc_text: Certo. E qual é o motivo? Para que é que precisa dele?
nodes.npc_translation: Right. And what's the reason? What do you need it for?

options.text: É para a escola do meu filho, para a inscrição.
options.translation: It's for my son's school, for registration.
options.feedback: Perfect, clear and concise explanation.

options.text: Preciso para a escola, para o meu filho.
options.translation: I need it for school, for my son.
options.feedback: Understood, but 'para a inscrição' (for registration) adds more precision.

nodes.npc_text: Um atestado de residência, certo? E para que é que precisa dele?
nodes.npc_translation: A certificate of residency, right? And what do you need it for?

options.text: Sim, isso mesmo. É para a escola do meu filho, para a inscrição.
options.translation: Yes, that's right. It's for my son's school, for registration.
options.feedback: Good job correcting yourself and providing the full reason.

nodes.npc_text: Ah, para a escola. Entendido. Então, para isso, vai precisar do seu cartão de cidadão ou título de residência e de um comprovativo de morada, como uma fatura da água, luz ou telefone, em seu nome.
nodes.npc_translation: Ah, for school. Understood. So, for that, you'll need your citizen card or residence permit and proof of address, like a water, electricity, or phone bill, in your name.

options.text: Tenho aqui o meu título de residência e um recibo da água.
options.translation: I have my residence permit and a water bill here.
options.feedback: Excellent! You have the correct documents.

options.text: Só tenho o meu passaporte e um contrato de arrendamento.
options.translation: I only have my passport and a rental agreement.
options.feedback: The passport is fine for ID, but a rental agreement isn't always accepted as proof of address. Be prepared for a follow-up question.

nodes.npc_text: O passaporte serve para a identificação. Quanto ao comprovativo de morada, o contrato de arrendamento pode não ser suficiente. Não tem uma fatura recente da água ou da luz em seu nome?
nodes.npc_translation: The passport works for identification. As for proof of address, the rental agreement might not be enough. Don't you have a recent water or electricity bill in your name?

options.text: Ah, não. Não tenho. Posso trazer amanhã?
options.translation: Oh, no. I don't. Can I bring it tomorrow?
options.feedback: Good, you're asking for a solution. This is a common real-life scenario.

options.text: Não, só tenho mesmo o contrato.
options.translation: No, I only have the contract.
options.feedback: You might need to find another document. The attendant will likely advise you to return.

nodes.npc_text: Pois, sem um comprovativo de morada aceite, não consigo emitir o atestado. Se tiver a fatura da água ou da luz, pode vir amanhã. O atendimento é das 9h às 16h.
nodes.npc_translation: Well, without an accepted proof of address, I can't issue the certificate. If you have the water or electricity bill, you can come tomorrow. Service is from 9 am to 4 pm.

options.text: Está bem, muito obrigado. Volto amanhã.
options.translation: Alright, thank you very much. I'll come back tomorrow.
options.feedback: You've successfully navigated the situation, even with a minor setback. Good politeness.

nodes.npc_text: Ótimo! Preencha este pequeno requerimento, faz favor. Em cinco minutos está pronto.
nodes.npc_translation: Great! Please fill out this small form. It will be ready in five minutes.

options.text: Muito obrigado/a!
options.translation: Thank you very much!
options.feedback: Success! You got your certificate.

dialogues.title: Arranging a Plumber's Visit
dialogues.context: A cliente (client) calls a canalizador (plumber) to arrange a visit for a leak in her kitchen.

lines.speaker: Canalizador
lines.text: Boa tarde, Canalizações Silva. Diga!
lines.translation: Good afternoon, Silva Plumbing. Speak!

lines.speaker: Cliente
lines.text: Boa tarde, sim. Estou a ligar por causa de uma fuga na cozinha. Precisava de um orçamento.
lines.translation: Good afternoon, yes. I'm calling about a leak in the kitchen. I needed a quote.

lines.speaker: Canalizador
lines.text: Ah, pois. Certo. Que dia lhe dá mais jeito para eu ir ver?
lines.translation: Ah, right. Okay. Which day is more convenient for you for me to go take a look?

lines.speaker: Cliente
lines.text: Hmm, pode ser quarta-feira de manhã ou quinta à tarde, talvez?
lines.translation: Hmm, could be Wednesday morning or Thursday afternoon, maybe?

lines.speaker: Canalizador
lines.text: Quinta à tarde dá-me jeito. Por volta das três, pode ser?
lines.translation: Thursday afternoon suits me. Around three, can it be?

lines.speaker: Cliente
lines.text: Sim, sim, às três está ótimo. Qual é a morada?
lines.translation: Yes, yes, three is great. What's the address?

lines.speaker: Canalizador
lines.text: É na Rua do Limoeiro, número vinte e cinco. No Funchal, claro.
lines.translation: It's on Rua do Limoeiro, number twenty-five. In Funchal, of course.

lines.speaker: Cliente
lines.text: Certo. Rua do Limoeiro, vinte e cinco. Fico à espera na quinta-feira, então.
lines.translation: Right. Rua do Limoeiro, twenty-five. I'll wait on Thursday, then.

lines.speaker: Canalizador
lines.text: P'ra já. Até quinta. Obrigado.
lines.translation: For now. See you Thursday. Thank you.

cultural_notes.title: Flexibility in Scheduling
cultural_notes.body: When arranging visits in Madeira, especially with small businesses or independent professionals, there's often flexibility. It's common for them to propose a time 'por volta das...' (around...) rather than a precise minute. Likewise, offering a couple of options for days/times shows consideration.

cultural_notes.title: Confirming the Address
cultural_notes.body: It's standard practice to reconfirm the full address, even if you think the person might have it. This avoids misunderstandings and ensures the professional arrives at the correct location. Using 'Qual é a morada?' or 'Pode confirmar a morada?' is polite and clear.

roleplay.scenario: You need a local handyman (o 'faz-tudo') to come to your house in Funchal to look at a broken gate and give you a quote. You call him to schedule a visit.

nodes.npc_text: Bom dia, 'Faz-Tudo' Manuel. Diga!
nodes.npc_translation: Good morning, 'Handyman' Manuel. Speak!

options.text: Bom dia. Estou a ligar porque preciso de um orçamento para um portão.
options.translation: Good morning. I'm calling because I need a quote for a gate.
options.feedback: Great start, clearly stating your purpose.

nodes.npc_text: Um portão, pois. Certo. Que dia lhe dá mais jeito para eu ir lá ver?
nodes.npc_translation: A gate, right. Okay. Which day is more convenient for you for me to go there and see?

options.text: Pode ser na terça-feira de manhã ou na quinta-feira à tarde?
options.translation: Could it be Tuesday morning or Thursday afternoon?
options.feedback: Good, offering two options is polite and helpful.

options.text: Não sei. Que dias tem disponíveis?
options.translation: I don't know. Which days do you have available?
options.feedback: This is okay, but it puts the burden on the other person. Try to offer options if you can.

nodes.npc_text: Quinta-feira à tarde, por volta das quatro, dá-me jeito. Pode ser?
nodes.npc_translation: Thursday afternoon, around four, suits me. Can it be?

options.text: Sim, às quatro na quinta-feira está ótimo. Qual é a morada?
options.translation: Yes, four on Thursday is great. What's the address?
options.feedback: Excellent, confirming the time and asking for the address.

options.text: Às quatro é um bocado tarde. Pode ser mais cedo?
options.translation: Four is a bit late. Can it be earlier?
options.feedback: Good, you're negotiating the time if it doesn't suit you.

nodes.npc_text: Tenho disponibilidade na terça-feira de manhã ou na quinta-feira à tarde. Que horas lhe dão mais jeito?

nodes.npc_text: Mais cedo? Hum, às três e meia, talvez? Ou só na sexta-feira de manhã?

dialogues.title: Renovation Scope Change
dialogues.context: A contractor (João) is explaining to a client (Dona Fátima) that a bathroom renovation project has encountered an unexpected problem, requiring extra work and cost.

lines.speaker: João
lines.text: Dona Fátima, tenho que lhe dar uma notícia sobre a obra na casa de banho.
lines.translation: Dona Fátima, I have to give you some news about the work in the bathroom.

lines.speaker: Dona Fátima
lines.text: Diga, João. Está tudo bem?
lines.translation: Tell me, João. Is everything alright?

lines.speaker: João
lines.text: Pois, o trabalho inicialmente acordado era só substituir os azulejos, mas agora descobrimos que há um problema sério de humidade na parede por trás. Está muito pior do que pensávamos.
lines.translation: Well, the work initially agreed upon was just to replace the tiles, but now we've discovered a serious damp problem in the wall behind. It's much worse than we thought.

lines.speaker: Dona Fátima
lines.text: Ai, meu Deus! Isso é mau. E o que é que isso implica?
lines.translation: Oh, my God! That's bad. And what does that imply?

lines.speaker: João
lines.text: Para resolver, vamos ter de tratar a humidade, o que vai implicar um custo adicional de cerca de 300 euros e mais uns dois ou três dias de trabalho. É um imprevisto, infelizmente.
lines.translation: To solve it, we'll have to treat the damp, which will imply an additional cost of about 300 euros and another two or three days of work. It's an unforeseen event, unfortunately.

lines.speaker: Dona Fátima
lines.text: 300 euros? E mais dias? O orçamento já estava um bocado apertado...
lines.translation: 300 euros? And more days? The budget was already a bit tight...

lines.speaker: João
lines.text: Entendo a sua preocupação, Dona Fátima, mas é essencial para que o trabalho fique bem feito e não tenha problemas no futuro. Precisamos da sua aprovação para avançar com esta parte extra.
lines.translation: I understand your concern, Dona Fátima, but it's essential for the work to be well done and to avoid future problems. We need your approval to move forward with this extra part.

cultural_notes.title: Addressing Service Providers and Clients
cultural_notes.body: In Portugal, when addressing service providers (like a contractor) or clients, it's common to use 'o senhor' / 'a senhora' (the polite third person) or their first name if the relationship is more established, often preceded by 'Sr.' / 'Sra.' or 'Dona' / 'Dom' for older individuals or those with higher status. Using 'você' is generally avoided in direct address as it can sound distancing or even rude in some contexts, especially when addressing someone older or in a professional setting. Instead, use the third person verb conjugation (e.g., 'o senhor pode' instead of 'você pode').

cultural_notes.title: Explaining Unexpected Costs
cultural_notes.body: When explaining unexpected costs or delays, it's important to be clear, justify the necessity, and present it as an 'imprevisto' (unforeseen event) rather than a mistake. Offering solutions and seeking explicit approval before proceeding shows professionalism and respect for the client's budget and time. Be prepared for some negotiation or expressions of concern, as budgets are often tight.

roleplay.scenario: You are a small contractor. You've been hired to fix a leaky tap in a client's kitchen. Upon inspection, you discover that the entire pipework behind the wall is corroded and needs replacing, which is a much bigger job than initially quoted. You need to explain this to the client, Mrs. Silva, explain the extra cost and time, and get her approval to proceed.

nodes.npc_text: Bom dia, Sr. {learner_name}. Já conseguiu ver o problema da torneira?
nodes.npc_translation: Good morning, Mr./Ms. {learner_name}. Have you managed to see the tap problem?

options.text: Sim, Dona Silva. O problema é mais sério do que pensávamos. A canalização atrás da parede está corroída.
options.translation: Yes, Dona Silva. The problem is more serious than we thought. The pipework behind the wall is corroded.
options.feedback: Good start, you've identified the core issue directly.

options.text: A torneira está estragada, mas podemos consertar.
options.translation: The tap is broken, but we can fix it.
options.feedback: This option doesn't address the new, bigger problem. You need to explain the scope escalation.

nodes.npc_text: Corroída? Ai, que chatice! E o que é que isso significa para a reparação?
nodes.npc_translation: Corroded? Oh, what a nuisance! And what does that mean for the repair?

options.text: Significa que não podemos só trocar a torneira. Temos de substituir toda a canalização, o que implica mais trabalho e materiais.
options.translation: It means we can't just change the tap. We have to replace all the pipework, which implies more work and materials.
options.feedback: Excellent, you're clearly explaining the extra work required.

options.text: Não se preocupe, eu dou um jeito.
options.translation: Don't worry, I'll figure it out.
options.feedback: This is too vague and doesn't explain the implications for the client. You need to be transparent about the changes.

nodes.npc_text: Entendo. E isso vai custar mais dinheiro e demorar mais tempo, não é?
nodes.npc_translation: I understand. And that will cost more money and take more time, won't it?

options.text: Sim, infelizmente. Vai implicar um custo adicional de 250 euros e mais uns dois dias de trabalho. É um imprevisto que não podíamos prever.
options.translation: Yes, unfortunately. It will imply an additional cost of 250 euros and about two more days of work. It's an unforeseen event we couldn't predict.
options.feedback: You've given the specifics clearly and acknowledged it's an unforeseen issue.

options.text: Não muito mais, talvez 50 euros e algumas horas.
options.translation: Not much more, maybe 50 euros and a few hours.
options.feedback: Be honest about the full scope. Underestimating now will lead to bigger problems later.

nodes.npc_text: 250 euros e dois dias? Ai, Sr. {learner_name}, o meu orçamento já está muito apertado. Não há outra solução?
nodes.npc_translation: 250 euros and two days? Oh, Mr./Ms. {learner_name}, my budget is already very tight. Is there no other solution?

options.text: Entendo perfeitamente a sua preocupação. Mas para garantir que a fuga não volta e que a canalização aguenta, esta é a única solução duradoura. Precisamos da sua aprovação para avançar.
options.translation: I perfectly understand your concern. But to ensure the leak doesn't return and the pipework holds up, this is the only lasting solution. We need your approval to proceed.
options.feedback: You've empathized, justified the necessity, and asked for approval directly.

options.text: Não sei, Dona Silva. É o que tem de ser.
options.translation: I don't know, Dona Silva. It's what has to be done.
options.feedback: This response is unhelpful and lacks empathy. Try to justify the necessity and offer reassurance.

nodes.npc_text: Pois é... Se diz que é a única forma de ficar bem feito... Está bem, Sr. {learner_name}. Pode avançar. Mas tente despachar-se, por favor.
nodes.npc_translation: Well... If you say it's the only way to do it right... All right, Mr./Ms. {learner_name}. You can proceed. But try to be quick, please.

options.text: Muito obrigado, Dona Silva. Vou fazer o meu melhor para ser o mais rápido possível.
options.translation: Thank you very much, Dona Silva. I'll do my best to be as quick as possible.
options.feedback: Great! You've secured approval and reassured the client. This is a successful outcome.

options.text: Certo. Não se preocupe.
options.translation: Okay. Don't worry.
options.feedback: While correct, a more polite and reassuring response would be better here, acknowledging her request for speed.

dialogues.title: Painting a Room
dialogues.context: A neighbour, Dona Laura, asks a local painter, Sr. Manuel, for a quote to paint a room in her house.

lines.speaker: Dona Laura
lines.text: Bom dia, Sr. Manuel. Tudo bem?
lines.translation: Good morning, Mr. Manuel. How are you?

lines.speaker: Sr. Manuel
lines.text: Bom dia, Dona Laura! Tudo bem, obrigado. Em que posso ajudar?
lines.translation: Good morning, Dona Laura! I'm fine, thank you. How can I help?

lines.speaker: Dona Laura
lines.text: Olhe, gostava de pedir um orçamento para pintar o quarto das visitas. É um bocado grande.
lines.translation: Look, I'd like to ask for a quote to paint the guest room. It's a bit big.

lines.speaker: Sr. Manuel
lines.text: Ah, sim. E já tem as tintas, ou é para eu tratar de tudo?
lines.translation: Ah, yes. And do you already have the paints, or should I take care of everything?

lines.speaker: Dona Laura
lines.text: Não, não. Seria para incluir tudo, p'ra ser mais fácil. E o que é que está incluído no orçamento?
lines.translation: No, no. It would be to include everything, to make it easier. And what's included in the quote?

lines.speaker: Sr. Manuel
lines.text: Inclui a preparação das paredes, a pintura, e a limpeza no final. A mão de obra e o material, claro.
lines.translation: It includes wall preparation, painting, and cleaning at the end. Labour and materials, of course.

lines.speaker: Dona Laura
lines.text: Pois, faz sentido. E para quando é que teria disponibilidade para fazer o trabalho?
lines.translation: Yes, that makes sense. And when would you be available to do the job?

lines.speaker: Sr. Manuel
lines.text: Consigo ir lá na próxima semana, talvez na terça ou quarta. Tenho de ver a minha agenda.
lines.translation: I can go there next week, maybe Tuesday or Wednesday. I need to check my schedule.

lines.speaker: Dona Laura
lines.text: Certo. E como é que funciona o pagamento? É no final?
lines.translation: Right. And how does payment work? Is it at the end?

lines.speaker: Sr. Manuel
lines.text: Normalmente peço um adiantamento de vinte por cento para o material, e o restante no final, depois de o serviço estar concluído.
lines.translation: Normally I ask for a twenty percent advance for materials, and the rest at the end, after the service is completed.

lines.speaker: Dona Laura
lines.text: Ah, ok. Percebi. Então, fico à espera do seu contacto com os detalhes. Obrigada!
lines.translation: Ah, okay. I understand. So, I'll wait for your contact with the details. Thank you!

lines.speaker: Sr. Manuel
lines.text: De nada, Dona Laura. Eu ligo-lhe amanhã, sem falta. Até já!
lines.translation: You're welcome, Dona Laura. I'll call you tomorrow, without fail. See you!

cultural_notes.title: Asking for a Quote
cultural_notes.body: In Portugal, it is very common and expected to ask for a quote ('orçamento') before committing to a service or purchase, especially for home repairs, renovations, or custom work. Don't hesitate to ask for details on what is included (materials, labour, taxes) and the timeframe.

cultural_notes.title: Payment Methods
cultural_notes.body: While cash is still widely accepted, MBWay is a popular mobile payment system used by many individuals and small businesses in Portugal for quick and easy transfers. For larger sums, bank transfers ('transferência bancária') are common. It's also normal for service providers to ask for an advance payment ('adiantamento') for materials, with the rest paid upon completion of the service.

roleplay.scenario: You need a handyman to fix a leaky tap in your bathroom. You call a local number you got from a neighbour.

nodes.npc_text: Olá! Fala o Zé, o canalizador. Em que posso ajudar?

options.text: Olá, Sr. Zé. Gostaria de pedir um orçamento para um arranjo.
options.translation: Hello, Mr. Zé. I'd like to ask for a quote for a repair.
options.feedback: Good start! You clearly state your intention.

nodes.npc_text: Pois não. Qual é o problema?

options.text: Tenho uma torneira a pingar na casa de banho.

options.text: É uma fuga de água na canalização da cozinha.

options.feedback: Good alternative, describing a different common plumbing issue.

nodes.npc_text: Uma torneira a pingar, hum... Isso é um arranjo simples, mas tenho de ver. Quando é que estaria disponível para eu ir lá ver?
nodes.npc_translation: A dripping tap, hmm... That's a simple repair, but I need to see it. When would you be available for me to go there and take a look?

options.text: Para quando é que tem disponibilidade? Eu tenho flexibilidade.
options.translation: When are you available? I have flexibility.
options.feedback: Good, you're asking about his availability.

options.text: Poderia vir amanhã de manhã?
options.translation: Could you come tomorrow morning?

nodes.npc_text: Amanhã de manhã está um bocado complicado. Quinta-feira à tarde, por exemplo? Ou sexta de manhã?
nodes.npc_translation: Tomorrow morning is a bit complicated. Thursday afternoon, for example? Or Friday morning?

options.text: Quinta-feira à tarde é perfeito para mim. Quanto é que custa a visita?

options.feedback: Excellent, you confirm the time and ask about the cost of the visit.

options.text: Sexta-feira de manhã dá-me jeito. A visita para o orçamento tem algum custo?
options.translation: Friday morning suits me. Does the visit for the quote have a cost?
options.feedback: Good, you confirm the time and ask about the cost of the quote visit.

nodes.npc_text: Não, não, a visita para o orçamento não tem custo. Só se o arranjo for feito no momento, claro. Então, fica para quinta à tarde?
nodes.npc_translation: No, no, the visit for the quote has no cost. Only if the repair is done on the spot, of course. So, it's set for Thursday afternoon?

options.text: Sim, fica combinado. Quinta-feira à tarde. Obrigada!
options.translation: Yes, it's agreed. Thursday afternoon. Thank you!

options.text: Perfeito. Fico à espera. Até quinta!
options.translation: Perfect. I'll be waiting. See you Thursday!
options.feedback: You've confirmed the appointment. Well done!

dialogues.title: Chasing Payment by Phone
dialogues.context: A small business owner in Funchal calls a client about an overdue invoice.

lines.text: Bom dia, senhor Silva. É da Loja do Zé. Estou a ligar sobre a fatura 2024/007.

cultural_notes.title: Politeness in Business Communication
cultural_notes.body: In Portugal, even when chasing payment, it's important to maintain a polite and respectful tone, especially when addressing someone formally (using 'o senhor'/'a senhora'). Direct confrontation is generally avoided. Phrases like 'Gostaria de saber sobre...' (I would like to know about...) or 'Estou a ligar sobre...' (I am calling about...) are good ways to introduce the topic indirectly before stating the problem.

cultural_notes.title: Handling Delays and Excuses
cultural_notes.body: It's common for clients to offer an excuse for late payment. Acknowledge their apology briefly ('Não há problema' - No problem) but then steer the conversation back to the payment itself, focusing on agreeing a new date. Avoid getting drawn into lengthy discussions about their reasons.

roleplay.scenario: You are calling a client, 'Sr. Fernandes', about an invoice (invoice number 12345) for 250 euros that is two weeks overdue. You need to politely remind him and agree on a new payment date.

nodes.npc_text: Olá, sou o Sr. Fernandes. Diga!
nodes.npc_translation: Hello, I'm Mr. Fernandes. Speak!

options.text: Bom dia, senhor Fernandes. É da [Nome da Sua Empresa]. Estou a ligar sobre a fatura 12345.
options.translation: Good morning, Mr. Fernandes. It's from [Your Company Name]. I'm calling about invoice 12345.
options.feedback: Excellent, polite and clear. Remember to state your company name for context.

options.text: Onde está o pagamento da fatura 12345?
options.translation: Where is the payment for invoice 12345?
options.feedback: A bit too direct for a first approach in Portuguese business culture. Try being more polite.

nodes.npc_text: Ah, a fatura 12345! Peço desculpa, esqueci-me completamente. Tive uma semana muito atarefada.
nodes.npc_translation: Ah, invoice 12345! I apologize, I completely forgot. I've had a very busy week.

options.text: Não há problema, senhor Fernandes. O valor em dívida é de duzentos e cinquenta euros. Quando é que pensa liquidar?
options.translation: No problem, Mr. Fernandes. The outstanding amount is two hundred and fifty euros. When do you plan to settle it?
options.feedback: Well done, you acknowledged his apology but immediately moved to the core issue and the amount.

options.text: Sim, eu sei. Mas preciso do dinheiro.
options.translation: Yes, I know. But I need the money.
options.feedback: This is still a bit confrontational. Try to be more collaborative in finding a solution.

nodes.npc_text: Hmm... consigo pagar no final da próxima semana, talvez sexta-feira, dia vinte e seis. Pode ser?
nodes.npc_translation: Hmm... I can pay by the end of next week, maybe Friday, the twenty-sixth. Is that okay?

options.text: Sim, sexta-feira, dia vinte e seis, está ótimo. Agradeço a sua colaboração, senhor Fernandes.
options.translation: Yes, Friday, the twenty-sixth, is great. I appreciate your cooperation, Mr. Fernandes.
options.feedback: Perfect! You confirmed the date and ended the call politely.

options.text: Não, preciso que pague amanhã.
options.translation: No, I need you to pay tomorrow.
options.feedback: This is unrealistic and too demanding. Try to be flexible and find a mutually agreeable date.

cultural_notes.title: Formality in Professional Introductions
cultural_notes.body: In professional networking contexts in Portugal, especially among peers or at less formal events, using 'tu' (informal 'you') is common. However, with older individuals, in very formal settings, or when addressing someone in a position of authority, 'o senhor/a senhora' (formal 'you') is used to show respect. Pay attention to the context and the other person's lead.

cultural_notes.title: Asking About Work
cultural_notes.body: It's perfectly normal and expected to ask 'O que fazes?' (What do you do?) or 'Qual é a tua área?' (What's your field?) early in an introduction at professional events. This is a standard way to start a conversation and understand common ground for networking.

options.feedback: Great start! Remember to use 'o' for male names and 'a' for female names after 'sou' if applicable.

nodes.npc_text: Ah, que interessante! [A SUA ÁREA PROFISSIONAL], pois... E o que fazes exatamente nessa área?

cultural_notes.title: Addressing the Taxi Driver
cultural_notes.body: In Portugal, it's common and polite to address service staff, including taxi drivers, using 'o senhor' (the sir) or 'a senhora' (the madam) rather than 'você' (you), which can sound overly direct or even distancing. A simple 'Boa tarde' or 'Olá' followed by your request is a good start.

cultural_notes.title: Confirming the Fare
cultural_notes.body: It's always a good idea to ask 'Quanto é?' (How much is it?) or 'Quanto custa?' (How much does it cost?) before or shortly after stating your destination, especially for longer trips like from the airport. Tipping is not mandatory but appreciated for good service; rounding up the fare is a common practice.

cultural_notes.title: Politeness in Service Interactions
cultural_notes.body: In Portugal, 'Queria...' (I would like...) is often preferred over 'Eu quero...' (I want...) when making a request, as it sounds more polite and less demanding. Adding 'por favor' or 'se faz favor' (please) is also essential for polite interactions in shops, cafés, and with service staff.

cultural_notes.title: Madeira Bus Zones (Horários do Funchal)
cultural_notes.body: Public transport in Funchal (operated by Horários do Funchal) uses a zone system. When buying a ticket on the bus, you'll need to specify which zone you're travelling to. The price increases with the number of zones. If you plan to use the bus frequently, consider buying a 'Giro' card and topping it up at a Horários do Funchal kiosk.

cultural_notes.title: Bringing a Gift
cultural_notes.body: When invited to someone's home for dinner in Madeira (and Portugal generally), it's customary to bring a small gift for the hosts. This could be a bottle of wine, a dessert, a box of chocolates, or flowers. Even if the host says 'não é preciso' (it's not necessary), it's a polite gesture to still bring something modest. It shows appreciation for their hospitality.

cultural_notes.title: Table Compliments
cultural_notes.body: It's common and appreciated to compliment the food, the host's cooking, and the overall atmosphere. Phrases like 'Está tudo delicioso!', 'A comida está ótima!', or 'Estou a gostar imenso da noite!' (I'm really enjoying the evening!) are well-received. You might also ask about a specific dish or ingredient, showing genuine interest.

roleplay.scenario: Your neighbour, Dona Fátima, stops you in the street and invites you for dinner. Later, you are at her house, making small talk.

nodes.npc_text: Olá! Tudo bem? Queríamos convidar-te para jantar cá a casa no sábado. Podes vir?

options.text: Sim, claro! Muito obrigado/a pelo convite! Posso levar alguma coisa?

nodes.npc_text: Que bom! P'ra aí às oito e meia. Não é preciso trazer nada, só a tua boa disposição!
nodes.npc_translation: Great! Around half past eight. No need to bring anything, just your good mood!

options.text: Ah, pois é! Mas posso levar uma sobremesa ou um vinho?

options.feedback: Good persistence! It's polite to insist a little.

options.feedback: Perfectly acceptable. Sometimes it's best to respect their wish not to bring anything.

nodes.npc_text: Ah, se insistes, podes trazer um vinho ou uma sobremesa. Mas não te preocupes, a sério!
nodes.npc_translation: Ah, if you insist, you can bring a wine or a dessert. But don't worry, seriously!

options.text: Perfeito, levo um vinho. Até sábado!
options.translation: Perfect, I'll bring a wine. See you Saturday!
options.feedback: Great! You confirmed what you'll bring and said goodbye.

options.text: Muito obrigado/a, Dona Fátima! Até sábado!
options.translation: Thank you very much, Dona Fátima! See you Saturday!
options.feedback: Good! You thanked her and confirmed. You can choose not to bring anything if she insists.

nodes.npc_text: (Later, at dinner) Então, o que é que achas do nosso bolo do caco com manteiga de alho? É uma especialidade daqui.
nodes.npc_translation: (Later, at dinner) So, what do you think of our bolo do caco with garlic butter? It's a specialty from here.

options.text: Está delicioso, Dona Fátima! E as semilhas, ui, que boas!
options.translation: It's delicious, Dona Fátima! And the potatoes, oh, how good!
options.feedback: Excellent! You gave a specific compliment and even added a Madeiran touch with 'semilhas'.

options.text: Está tudo muito bom! Que refeição fantástica!
options.translation: Everything is very good! What a fantastic meal!
options.feedback: Good general compliment. You expressed your enjoyment clearly.

nodes.npc_text: Que bom que gostas! É uma receita antiga da minha avó. Queres mais um bocado de poncha?
nodes.npc_translation: Good that you like it! It's an old recipe from my grandmother. Do you want a little more poncha?

options.text: Sim, por favor! Está tudo ótimo, muito obrigado/a!
options.translation: Yes, please! Everything is great, thank you very much!
options.feedback: Perfect way to end the conversation, accepting the offer and thanking them again.

options.text: Não, obrigado/a. Mas estou a gostar imenso da conversa e da companhia!
options.translation: No, thank you. But I'm really enjoying the conversation and the company!
options.feedback: Polite refusal and a lovely compliment about the company. Well done!

sit-social-making-plans-weekend.title: Making Weekend Plans
sit-social-making-plans-weekend.summary: Arranging to meet up: suggesting a levada walk or a coffee, agreeing a time and place, and confirming by message.

phrase_patterns.base: O que é que te apetece fazer {time_frame}?
phrase_patterns.translation: What do you feel like doing {time_frame}?

variants.translation: Do you feel like going for a walk?

slots.name: time
slots.description: A specific time or day part

variants.translation: What time suits you?

phrase_patterns.translation: I'll send a message to confirm later.

vocabulary.note: Madeira regionalism
Portugal regionalism

dialogues.title: Planning a Levada Walk
dialogues.context: Two friends, Maria and João, are chatting casually, planning something for the weekend in Madeira.

lines.speaker: João
lines.text: Então, Maria, o que é que te apetece fazer este fim de semana?
lines.translation: So, Maria, what do you feel like doing this weekend?

lines.speaker: Maria
lines.text: Hmm... Apetece-me ir dar um passeio. Que tal uma levada?
lines.translation: Hmm... I feel like going for a walk. How about a levada?

lines.speaker: João
lines.text: Boa ideia! A da Ribeira da Janela, talvez? É bonita.
lines.translation: Good idea! The one at Ribeira da Janela, maybe? It's beautiful.

lines.speaker: Maria
lines.text: Sim, pode ser. Sábado de manhã, às nove e meia?
lines.translation: Yes, that can be. Saturday morning, at half past nine?

lines.speaker: João
lines.text: P'ra mim, tá ótimo! Encontramo-nos lá à entrada?
lines.translation: For me, that's great! Shall we meet there at the entrance?

lines.speaker: Maria
lines.text: Combinado! Depois mando mensagem a confirmar tudo.
lines.translation: Agreed! I'll send a message to confirm everything later.

cultural_notes.title: Levadas: Madeira's Unique Footpaths
cultural_notes.body: Levadas are ancient irrigation channels unique to Madeira, originally built to carry water from the north to the drier south of the island. Today, many have become popular walking trails, offering stunning views and a chance to experience Madeira's natural beauty. When suggesting activities, a 'levada walk' is a very common and appreciated option.

cultural_notes.title: Informal Planning with 'Tu'
cultural_notes.body: When making plans with friends or peers in Portugal, it's very common and natural to use the informal 'tu' form. Expressions like 'apetece-te' (do you feel like), 'que horas te dão jeito' (what time suits you), and 'encontramo-nos' (shall we meet) are standard for casual arrangements. Confirming via message ('mando mensagem a confirmar') is also typical after a phone call.

roleplay.scenario: You are calling a friend in Madeira to make plans for the upcoming weekend. You want to suggest either going for coffee or a walk.

nodes.npc_text: Olá! Tudo bem? O que é que se passa?
nodes.npc_translation: Hi! All good? What's up?

options.text: Queria combinar qualquer coisa para o fim de semana.
options.translation: I wanted to arrange something for the weekend.
options.feedback: Good, you've stated your intention clearly.

options.text: Estava a pensar no que fazer no sábado.
options.translation: I was thinking about what to do on Saturday.
options.feedback: This is also good, specifying the day.

nodes.npc_text: Ah, boa! O que é que te apetece?
nodes.npc_translation: Oh, good! What do you feel like doing?

options.text: Que tal irmos beber um café?
options.translation: How about we go for a coffee?
options.feedback: A simple and common suggestion. 'Irmos' is the correct informal form.

options.text: Apetece-me ir dar um passeio, talvez uma levada.
options.translation: I feel like going for a walk, maybe a levada.
options.feedback: Excellent, a very Madeiran suggestion!

nodes.npc_text: Sim, pode ser! Que horas te dão jeito?
nodes.npc_translation: Yes, that can be! What time suits you?

options.text: Sábado de manhã, às dez?
options.translation: Saturday morning, at ten?
options.feedback: Good, clear suggestion of day and time.

options.text: Domingo à tarde, pelas quatro?
options.translation: Sunday afternoon, around four?
options.feedback: Another good option, using 'pelas' for 'around'.

nodes.npc_text: Sim, adoro as levadas! Quando é que podíamos ir?
nodes.npc_translation: Yes, I love the levadas! When could we go?

options.text: Que tal sábado de manhã, bem cedo?
options.translation: How about Saturday morning, quite early?
options.feedback: Good, 'bem cedo' is a natural way to say 'quite early'.

options.text: Domingo à tarde, se não estiver muito calor?
options.translation: Sunday afternoon, if it's not too hot?
options.feedback: Excellent, adding a condition makes it very natural.

nodes.npc_text: Sim, essa hora é perfeita. Onde é que nos encontramos?
nodes.npc_translation: Yes, that time is perfect. Where shall we meet?

options.text: No café perto do mercado?
options.translation: At the café near the market?
options.feedback: Clear suggestion for a meeting point.

options.text: Naquela pastelaria nova?
options.translation: At that new pastry shop?
options.feedback: Good, using 'naquela' to refer to a known place.

nodes.npc_text: Sim, ótimo! Onde é que nos encontramos?
nodes.npc_translation: Yes, great! Where shall we meet?

options.text: À entrada da levada, como da última vez?
options.translation: At the entrance to the levada, like last time?
options.feedback: Good, referencing a previous meeting is natural.

options.text: Podíamos ir no meu carro. Eu apanho-te em casa.
options.translation: We could go in my car. I'll pick you up at home.
options.feedback: A practical suggestion, showing initiative.

nodes.npc_text: Perfeito! Então está combinado.
nodes.npc_translation: Perfect! So it's arranged.

options.text: Está combinado! Depois mando mensagem a confirmar.
options.translation: It's arranged! I'll send a message to confirm later.
options.feedback: Great, you've confirmed and mentioned the follow-up message.

options.text: Pois é! Até lá.
options.translation: Indeed! See you then.
options.feedback: A simple and friendly way to end the call.
